While the campus is praised for its beauty and the supportive atmosphere among staff and students, there are concerns regarding the high tuition costs , food options, and housing conditions. Overall, Chapman is seen as a solid choice for students looking for a personalized educational experience but may pose challenges in affordability and diversity.

reviews.text: I got in! I am still deciding whether to go! I hope to take a tour soon!! It is in Orange, California, so it should be very sunny. I heard the town is super nice.

Overall, my experience at Chapman University has been the epitome of what I expected college to be. I have had an amazing time adapting to this new phase of life and going through the successes and challenges of college. Chapman University provides an enriching environment where I can now call my home. I have enjoyed applying myself academically, engaging in new personal relationships, and going out of my comfort zone to build my own experience. Chapman has been nothing but welcoming to me and I am beyond thrilled to call it my home for the next four years.

Chapman is great! Especially the education program. Through this program you get to know your professors very well and create bonds with them and your peers. The class sizes are small which helps play a part in getting to know your professors.

Chapman University is a very welcoming and diverse, rich in academic variety. The housing is very nice, with ample space and ammenities such as a pool, water filling station, and lounges. There is a large variety of food opens at the cafeteria with options for everyone.

I’m a freshman and the experience has been really good. Everybody is welcoming and nice, especially the staff. Professors are amazing so far, helpful and kind, and definitely are knowledgeable within their fields. The surrounding area is nice, thrift stores and great food spots. I find the campus to be just the right size, it is also very walkable.
its been good; my classes have been interesting and stimulating and social life has been great. I've found lots of fun extracurriculars to do !

I really like Chapman University overall! I have enjoyed my classes and I have had good experiences with all my professors so far. What I like most about Chapman is the class size and how it really makes it so you can build connections and get to know your peers and professors more. I would like to see change on campus food as I feel like it consists of the same food every week so it get very repetitive.

Chapman felt like a place where I was genuinely supported. My professors always encouraged growth and made sure I felt seen, which made a huge difference in how confident I became with the coursework. I loved the small campus vibe since it made everything feel close and familiar. Plus, there were always lots of student activities happening, so it was easy to stay involved and feel connected to the community.
Chapman was the best choice I made. I chose the university because of Dodge and never regretted my choice. But I do wish they gave me more aid.

Chapman University offers a beautiful campus in the heart of Orange. Professors and academics have always been excellent. The cafeteria could be better but the campus has many different places to eat which balances it out. Socially, Chapman is a lively school but it tends to empty during the weekends.

Chapman is a small school located in residential area of orange. The campus has diverse student organizations and a beautiful campus. It's small size makes it easier to build relationships with professors that will aid you in the future with internships and research opportunities. Doge college of film is the 4th best film school in the nation. It has an amazing film course and offers opportunities to hear celebrities speak at their masterclass, in the past month we've had Spike lee, The Rock, Jeremy Allen white, and soon Ariana Grande. Chapman offers many opportunities if you are willing to put yourself out there

It’s definitely a great place to be if you are in the know on what to do. There’s no shortage of things to do if you know where to look, but it can be difficult or take a while to find what’s right for you to how to access it. There are a lot of opportunities that are exclusive/ hard to pursue, and that can be frustrating. Overall, the campus is really nice and most people are open minded and friendly. There is definitely a genuine sense of community and most people are always open to making new friends.

My overall experience at Chapman has been pretty good. Freshman year I had 3 roommates and had to deal with 6 girls all in one bathroom which was hell. There are a lot of problems at Chapman including mold, bees/wasps, ants, and etc. I would love if they changed the living situation at Chapman. The amount where paying does not suffice our living situation.

Chapman does an amazing job providing you with ample opportunities and resources. Whether you are interested in Greek life, professional networking, study abroad, or research, there is a place for you at Chapman. If I were changing something, I would encourage Chapman to cultivate a more involved student body at university sponsored events like athletics and performing arts. I would also like to see increased opportunities for Chapman studies to engage with the community, whether through recreation events, volunteering, or entry level employment.

Chapman’s Campus is very pretty and isn’t too large so everything is always a 5-15 minute walk from each other. Theres also a good amount of places to eat on campus and outside. The academics are great and the professors are always friendly and helpful. I really like how all my professors always want to really help their students and get to know and really connect with them as well. The housing that Chapman provides is pretty good and there’s always something going on. You can really meet a lot of people just by living on campus. There’s also a lot of financial aid given to students so I really appreciate that. They always have many resources for their students and their financial aid office is super helpful since they always make it very easy to understand what questions you have regarding anything to do with your financial aid, scholarships, etc.Outside of Chapman there is a very nice shopping area to relax and eat out so I definitely recommend checking everything out there!

ai_summary: Chatham University presents a mixed experience for students, with a notable emphasis on its beautiful campus and supportive community . Many reviews highlight the dedication and accessibility of professors, contributing to a strong academic environment , especially for those in small, discussion-based classes. Student life appears to be a point of contention; while some appreciate the close-knit atmosphere and campus activities, others find it lacking and more suited for academic pursuits than social engagement. The transition to co-education is acknowledged, but concerns about diversity and inclusion remain. The cost of attendance is frequently mentioned as a significant drawback , particularly in relation to housing and dining quality, which some describe as subpar. Overall, while Cougars find value in strong academic programs and supportive faculty, others express frustration over management issues and the campus culture.

reviews.text: My campus life at Chatham University has been a transformative blend of academic rigor and meaningful engagement. As an international student from Zambia, I've immersed myself in the vibrant intellectual community, consistently earning Dean's List recognition while maintaining a 3.49 GPA. My time as a Library Assistant allowed me to support fellow students while deepening my own research capabilities. Beyond coursework in Economics and Accounting, I've built strong mentoring relationships with professors who've guided my research on economic development. The experience of bridging my African heritage with American academia has enriched my perspective, preparing me to contribute meaningfully to global economic challenges.
Very accommodating and open to change and assisting wherever possible. Professors help as much as possible and are almost always available.

DO NOT GO HERE. so disorganized and unprofessional. the faculty will take forever to do anything and sometimes just lie to you about something being done or fixed because there is no communication amongst them. i transferred out and somehow the school still charged me and unconcensually took out loans in my name. this is disgusting and illegal. please do not go here, save yourself from so much stress and from potentially being scammed by people who refuse to do their jobs properly. the school is desperate for money because of their debt issue and will sacrifice the wellbeing of students in order to bail themselves out. please go to any other college.

Chatham University is a private, small university based in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. The campus is especially scenic in the fall and spring, featuring plenty of green areas and historic architecture. With small class sizes, students have the chance to build close connections with both peers and professors. Everyone really knows each other because of the small class sizes. Many faculty members are approachable and dedicated to helping students thrive. You can easily meet with professors.
I love how Chatham is part of Pittsburgh while still providing a sense of comfort and safety in a residential area.
It's a lovely campus tucked away in the middle of a city. The campus is safe, professors are incredibly passionate about what they teach, and are very happy to help you achieve your goals.

After spending the past year at Chatham, I have come to love many things about the College Experience. I loved the accepting community, different classes offered, and so much more. The only thing I hope to see change is the school letting students know their professors sooner if an adjunct is no longer teaching a class, or, if possible, better communication.

My daughter and I were told the PA program was a 5 year program and you were guaranteed a seat in their PA program. My daughters co-students were told the same. That is absolutely not the case. You still have to apply and go through the process as you were any one new to the university. You don’t even get put to the front of the list. We were misinformed on so many things. My advice is to have your advisors put everything they say into writing. If we would have known this information, we would have chosen a different university. The dorms are old, nasty, no air conditioning and co-ed bathrooms. The first day she took a shower, a male was allowed in the bathroom. My daughter decided to move back home and commute within the second week of being there. She tried to give it sometime, but realized quickly that it wasn’t for her. The whole experience was definitely not a “fun college experience “. I Wouldn’t recommend this expensive university and the fact that they weren’t honest.

Personally I had an amazing experience at Chatham. Although a bit expensive, it offers a wide range of courses and multiple resources to help the students succeed. The professors are also always available to help, and there are numerous organizations for students to join to help with their skills/resumes.

It's a small campus, which is both good and bad. You run into people you know, even if it's just from classes or happening to see them around a lot. This creates a feeling of closeness, but at the same time it means you have to leave campus for much activity. Fortunately there are multiple active neighborhoods within walking distance.
In my experience, it's been easy getting classes you want and you don't need to think about lots of backup options. Professors in my experience have been nice and approachable, but some classes can feel chaotic at times. My overall take is that if you want a small school with a community feel that's close to city amenities (shops, public transit, etc.) this might be a good choice for you.

Chatham has a very underrated Political Science program. The academics here are great, and it has a lovely campus. However, student life here definitely isn't for everyone. There's not much going on socially, and if you are a transfer, it can be hard to meet people.

I am a grad student here and truly love it!! There are so many good things to say about it. I am in the MSCP program and have enjoyed it a lot more than I thought I would! There are so many funding opportunities that I did not see at other schools with similar programs. All the faculty, staff, and students are so friendly, open, and welcoming. It is small, so professors know your name and you get to develop close relationships with them. There are opportunities to participate in various research projects as well. Any psych staff I've spoken to in Pittsburgh has been impressed with the program and it has a good rep. I truly think you or any other student reading this would like it!

Chatham Uni is an exceptional school when it comes to academics and general student life in dorms and on campus. The online system is absolutely terrible, the websites are incredibly outdated and difficult to use. The app we are told to use that can guide us through what we need like resident resources, dining menus, event schedules, and all else that a student might need (like safety or emergency help) forces you to sign in every. single. time. that an icon is clicked, so I have to sign in to the app, and then sign in again to go into any of the actual functions of the app- with authenticator! I know the RA's are treated terribly and the dining hall is out of food 80% of the day, even when open. I really think the academic part is wonderful, but if you plan on living here for more than your freshman year... think again.

I absolutely love attending Chatham University! It has been a pleasure working with all my professors and advisors from the very first time of me transferring from another University. They make everything easier and seamless no matter how much I worry. I really appreciate the opportunities I'm given here.

Very beautiful campus that can feel very small after a few years. The city is nice to have close by, but due to the size of the campus everyone starts to know everyone and it starts to adopt the feel of high school part 2.

ai_summary: Clark Atlanta University offers a vibrant and engaging campus life , especially for those looking for a culturally rich environment . Students appreciate the supportive community , with many highlighting the professors' dedication to their success and the numerous opportunities for networking and personal growth through various organizations. While the social atmosphere is lively, some feel that the campus dining options could be improved and that there are issues with housing and maintenance. The campus also benefits from its connection to the Atlanta University Center, providing a unique collaborative experience with nearby institutions. However, concerns about communication with the administration and campus safety have been noted, suggesting areas for improvement in student services. Overall, CAU is seen as a welcoming place for students , particularly those seeking an HBCU experience.

reviews.text: It was a great experience at Clark Atlanta University. The professors were great, and helpful. There was a diverse of students, faculty, and staff. Clark Atlanta University opened up many other doors for me. I received my Bachelor of Science degree in Religion from Clark Atlanta University. I was blessed to attend Clark Atlanta University.
I'd say It's been really good so far. it's very easy to communicate with like counselors and what not.

I earned my Doctorate in educational leadership from this university. I served as the student government association President for graduate studies. I served as an advisor to the SACS accreditation process.

I am now a sophomore at Clarke Atlanta University and so far I have deeply enjoyed my time there. Just like many other schools there have been the ups and downs dealing with professors, financial aid, student advisors, etc. Overall my experience has been worth it. My peers are determined and goal motivated and they help me push pass my limits as a student and think big. Many if not all of our professors are professionals within their field and teach with intent. I have left all my classes knowing a lot more than I did before. Outside of the classroom, whether it is a hangout in our dorms or a night walk to the city, it is always a great time. The city is about a 15-30 minute walk away from campus, there is always something to do. I love my school and the people I have met through my school, the connections are endless. I hope to continue my education at Clark and graduate with a high standing.

My experience at Clark Atlanta University was both enriching and memorable. I met friends from all over the world, which broadened my perspective and helped me appreciate diverse cultures and ideas. The faculty were extremely supportive and genuinely invested in my success, creating an environment where I felt encouraged to grow academically and personally. I learned a great deal inside and outside the classroom, gaining knowledge and skills that continue to shape me today. What made the experience even more special was that learning was balanced with fun through campus life, friendships, and shared experiences that made my time at Clark Atlanta not only educational, but truly enjoyable. Those connections, lessons, and memories continue to influence how I approach my career and relationships today.

Overall, my experience at Clark Atlanta University has been meaningful and rewarding. What I like most is the sense of community, school spirit, and the many opportunities to get involved on campus. I enjoy the culture, student organizations, and events like Homecoming, which really make CAU feel like home. I also appreciate how hands-on and supportive some departments are, especially business and fashion, because they make students feel guided and invested in.
What I don’t like is the lack of consistency and hands-on support in the Biology department. As a science student, I wish there was more engagement, mentorship, and direct academic guidance. I also dislike how limited the major options are for students who want to go into the health field. Additionally, communication and administrative processes like advisement, registration, and financial aid can feel overwhelming and unorganized at times, which adds unnecessary stress.

My journey here at Clark Atlanta University, has been transformative. I am originally from Inglewood, California, which meant that coming across the country for college as an undergraduate student definitely consisted of an adjustment. Life in the beginning was hectic, I cried, lost motivation, and struggled to navigate college life. As a freshman living off campus made things much more difficult to feel surrounded by community or even get involved/acclimated to campus life. Eventually I made friends, went to events, got involved and even started an organization that aligned with my college experience as well as my major.
However, it took time to get involved, it took time to understand how things work, but throughout my journey I wished that there were mentors given to you upon acceptance. Something like an older sister to give you guidance, I wish there were publicity and advertisement on how to get involved on campus. Overall, I enjoy my University, and with time it will evolve.

My experience at Clark Atlanta University has been amazing. The professors challenge and support students, helping me grow in psychology and community service. I’ve gained leadership skills through volunteering and learned the importance of giving back. Being part of a strong student community has helped me build connections and stay focused on my goals. Clark Atlanta has shaped me into a more confident, driven, and community-minded person, and it has prepared me well for the future.

My experience at Clark Atlanta University has been transformative, shaping my identity and boosting my confidence. CAU is more than a school; it fosters a drive to aim higher while honoring my roots. I've gained invaluable insights from ambitious peers and received mentorship from faculty that encourages personal growth. Balancing academics, athletics, and responsibilities has taught me resilience and the importance of perseverance. The supportive community celebrates achievements and helps navigate challenges. Overall, CAU has equipped me with knowledge and a sense of purpose, inspiring me to create a legacy that uplifts others. I'm truly grateful for this experience.

Clark Atlanta University is a Historically Black College and University (HBCU) with a strong legacy in Atlanta. It's known for its programs in business, mass media arts, and education. Students often praise the strong sense of community and the supportive environment that the university offers. Some common suggestions for improvement include upgrading facilities and increasing resources for academic programs.

My experience is a unique one because my first semester of college was dedicated to being in band. That made my experience pretty negative and hard to enjoy because I had no time to enjoy my campus because I was always on a trip or in practice. Come spring semester I quit being in band & really began to take everything in; campus life, cafeteria, extracurriculars, etc. I began to find myself beyond what I attended Clark Atlanta for.
Housing- We need and deserve way more visitation, we’re virtually allowed to have no one over.
Cafeteria- Sometimes the food is decent
Classes- I absolutely love my department and am learning so much. My classes are my favorite part about being here!
Public safety- they do their best to protect , however they failed me. I was and still am not properly protected from another student on campus due to cameras not working.

My overall experience at Clark Atlanta University has been both rewarding and challenging. I’ve grown a lot academically, socially, and personally since coming here. The community is very supportive, and I love how the campus encourages students to step outside their comfort zones and get involved in different opportunities. I’ve had the chance to hold leadership roles, network with professionals, and create meaningful relationships that will last a lifetime.
One thing I would like to see change is the communication and organization between departments, especially when it comes to student resources and financial aid. Sometimes it feels like students have to work extra hard to get the help they need. Still, CAU has given me valuable experiences that continue to shape me into the woman I want to become.
I have had a good undergrad experience. I was able to vertically and horizontally network and create a base of support for my professional career.

Well its been one week since i been at clark its been good experience so far. The only thing i dislike is getting help to pay for college if u cant get a cosign. But overall its been a good experience so far and i glad i made this choice

My journey here at Clark Atlanta University began August 2024. And from the moment I stepped foot into campus i felt, supported, I knew CAU was my home especially the Orientation Guides they made me feel welcomed, they made sure I had a good experience week so I know what my journey will look like. And going into my sophomore year now I can say Clark Atlanta University was the best decision I ever made.
I started getting involved just within a couple of months. I am involved in Spirit Boosters which is just supporting athletics, Her Fitness a sisterhood organization that works on health, growth, community serve. Her Campus CAU I am a part of the YouTube Team, Orientation Guides we bring in the incoming freshman, New Student Orientation, Freshman experience week. And honestly, I enjoy every organization I am in so far Here at Clark Atlanta University. I like the closeness my school brings, always trying to keep us safe. A change I would like to see is in the Residence Halls.

ai_summary: Clark University offers a unique blend of strong academics and close-knit community life, with many students highlighting the value of personal connections with professors . The academic programs, particularly in the sciences and liberal arts, are well-regarded, and there's a strong emphasis on research opportunities . However, frustrations about housing shortages and dining quality are prevalent among reviews, as many students have experienced stressful situations related to both. Social life appears to vary significantly, with some finding it welcoming and vibrant , while others describe it as stagnant, especially if connections aren't formed early. The campus is described as inclusive and diverse , although there are concerns about safety in the surrounding area. Students appreciate the unique culture of Clark, though some express dissatisfaction with the administration's responsiveness and the overall value compared to tuition costs. Overall, Clark University is seen as a great fit for motivated students seeking individualized learning and community engagement, but it may not cater to those wanting a more traditional college experience filled with parties and bustling activities.

reviews.text: Clark University is full of many wonderful people , from the staff to the professors almost all are very friendly and enthusiastic about help others. The university campus itself is noteworthy with a lot of beautiful greenery and well maintained building. One of the things I would love to see changes is the menu of the meals serviced there, as most of the food is lacking is flavor and nutrition.
My experience at Clark was relatively great. I like the vibrant campus and warm environment. I would like to have more variety of food options and more courses.

Clark was, for me, an intellectually stimulating environment with excellent support from faculty. The university has good resources and truly cares about its students and faculty. I chose it over Cornell, Colby, Vermont and Northeastern having been accepted to all of them. Attending a smaller research intensive university really made a difference for me. Some might prefer larger institutions and if so, Clark is probably not the place for you. Going to Clark changed my life and I still value the start it gave me in life. Professors challenged me in relatively small classes and the challenges were always helpful. I grew a lot.

Clark has amazing professors and academic offerings. Courses are engaging and promote real-world application. You will take so much away from your time at Clark, and the connections are endless. Every company I have looked into has had someone from Clark. You wouldn't expect it, but this small liberal arts school is making great strides and constantly improving their academic standings. Take advantage of public transit, go try some amazing authentic cuisine, and put yourself out there. Worcester is a great hub for colleges! If Clark doesn't have a specific course you are interested in, you can go to WPI or Holy Cross through the consortium (free). I highly recommend this school to anyone that thrives in a setting where professors know your name and can help you achieve your goals.

I have enjoyed my time at Clark thus far. There are always complaints which you will find at any University. At Clark, these are particularly about food and funding. It is hard to receive adequate funding for clubs and on-campus activities. However, financial aid and merit aid is extensive. Food is difficult, as is the case on many campuses, but Harvest Table (our catering group) is new and will hopefully pull themselves together.

Clark University prioritizes Academics and Diversity. They aim to give students a diverse course load by requiring courses in the humanities as well as the sciences. There are hundreds of clubs and School events to go to and make friends at, the Clark community is very welcoming. This hospitality is extended to professors at well, it's been my experience that the professors at Clark want you to succeed and work with you and are eager to help. Overall Clark feels like a big school but has the small hometown feel, you know the people there and they know you.

I think the professors here are great and the education is good. The location is unfortunately in a bad location in Worcester, however if you're able to leave campus that helps a bit. There isn't much of a party scene and it can feel cliquey. The food isn't great but once you can live off campus and cook it's much better.

As an upcoming third-year Computer Science student at Clark University, my experience is marred by a transitional period for the department. While Clark as a whole offers a welcoming campus and strong support systems, my classes outside of the games and art departments feel hamfisted, lacking soul.
The professors in the Computer Science department are, at best, serviceable. Most are approachable, and they’ll help you if you reach out, but few seem genuinely passionate about the material or their students’ long-term growth. Lectures often feel like they’re delivered out of obligation. The department lacks the energy or vision found in other CS programs.
This ambivalence even trickles down to the students. There’s a disconnect between coursework and student engagement. Many of my peers treat assignments as hurdles rather than opportunities to build skills. As such, Chat-GPT usage is rampant.
Aside from that, the housing is great, the food is excellent.

Clark offered students a great opportunity to explore many academic interests all in time to graduate. For those who are undecided, this lets you determine what you enjoy to study before committing to a major.

When I chose Clark, I envisioned a vibrant academic life in a city like Boston—full of culture and opportunity. However, Worcester isn’t Boston. It’s a smaller, developing city with limited public transportation and fewer off-campus options. That said, Clark offers strong academics—particularly in psychology, geography, and international development. The close-knit campus and accessible, supportive faculty create a personalized learning environment. Unique programs like the Fifth-Year Master’s add meaningful value. Socially, adjusting can be challenging, especially for international students, as the quieter, more segmented scene may sometimes feel isolating. Clark is ideal for those who value mentorship, small class sizes, and a slower, more intentional pace. But if you’re seeking the energy of a big city, it’s essential to research Worcester as thoroughly as Clark itself. For the right student, Clark can be deeply rewarding—just be sure it aligns with your expectations.

Clark had its ups and downs, but overall it was pretty good education. The professors are super cool and want to see you thrive, and the international student office here is SO supportive, even post grad.
Clark is a beautiful campus with a wonderful, welcoming community. When I first visited Clark, I felt at home. I really enjoy the small classes and campus size.

Clark University is a wonderful and supportive community of students. As a business major, I felt that the program was lacking a bit. The party scene is very little but it is nice to be surrounded by so many other schools so if you are connected you have more options.

No football, hockey or greek life which is fine by me! Parties feel like friends hanging out and sports/activities host parties. Professors are passionate about their students, at least in the sociology and psychology department
Small school with a variety of academic disciplines, while also offering a liberal arts core curriculum to give real perspective to undergrad students.

ai_summary: Clarke University offers a close-knit and welcoming community that many students appreciate, highlighting the small campus size which fosters personal connections with professors and peers. While the academic experience varies—some students express concerns about the effectiveness of certain programs—most agree that professors are approachable and willing to provide individualized assistance. The campus life appears to be supportive with numerous clubs, events, and a strong athletic culture, although some reviews mention that students who are not athletes may feel overlooked. Student services, such as the MARC for academic support, receive positive remarks. However, there are comments regarding outdated dorm facilities and high tuition costs , with a few students feeling that financial aid communication could be improved. Overall, Clarke University is viewed as a place that prioritizes student success within a familial environment, though there is a consistent call for more diverse academic offerings and better infrastructure.

reviews.text: I had a terrible experience at this school. From poorly designed courses, to bug filled environments, it was nearly impossible for me to focus on my assignments. For every friendly professor, their’s five narcissists that seem more interested in shouting orders rather than teach authentic lessons. Some of my accounting assignments were so bad, they left be depressed and exhausted. But what was easily the worst part of the experience, is that the school removed a ton of professors from the establishment. Including a good friend of my father’s. The best part about my graduation ceremony is that I’ll never have to enter this terrible school ever again.

My time at college was truly unforgettable! I had the opportunity to immerse myself in a vibrant campus community, forge lifelong friendships, and dive headfirst into academics that ignited my passion. From late-night study sessions and spirited football games to attending concerts and trying new hobbies, every moment was a chance to grow, learn, and make memories that l'll treasure forever - like that time my team won a basketball tournament.

I had a great time at this university. It was over all a great time, and even though I was away from my parents and house, Clark University made me feel at home. It such a harm and welcoming place. I dropped out because off money, but those two years I had there were the best. Everyone's so nice and kind. The living spaces are not amazing but they were livable. The food was okay, could be better.
I have loved my time at Clarke and would highly recommend it for a lot of people. the majors are limited but the programs available are amazing.

As an International student I didn't know what to expect going to school in the United States, Clarke University gave me a second home away from home. I've felt welcome from the very first day I got here. Through Clarke I've been able to make important connections that will help me in my professional life and friends that I will keep in contact with for the rest of my life. The best thing about Clarke are the people that work there!

I have completed my first year at Clarke and I absolutely loved it. The staff is fantastic and making sure you succeed. The campus and small and make it feel like home. There is so many students from different states and countries. Clarke really made my first year a great experience. I would love to see the dining halls be open earlier and longer on weekends.
I loved attending Clarke University. It was a great school with an amazing education program! I would highly recommend it to anyone.

Clarke University has given me an amazing opportunity to continue in playing the sport that I love and also receiving my education. The staff here at Clarke also created a wonderful environment for me to feel welcome and a part of a community.

I was a no. Traditionally student. Some of the classes were wonderful with my different circumstances and others made me feel terrible about being a single mother. I was even told I wouldn’t be able to make it while having a child at home.

Clarke is a one of a kind small college that embodies a close community style. For me this was perfect and I really enjoyed personally knowing my professors and peers. Something that negatively affected me was the lack of pre-physical therapy majors. In attending Clarke I was able to finish my undergraduate degree early, but they do not have exercise science or kinesiology (the top 2 majors for physical therapy).
Everyone wants the best for you. Very tight community everyone knows each other. I love the small atmosphere since I came from a small high school.

I transferred to Clarke just this semester (Fall 2023). I was drawn to them by their BSN program, which I have heard from many others is amazing. The staff is amazing and take time to help you if you need it.

I have enjoyed my time here so far, I played on the men's golf team this past fall and will again in the spring when it gets warm again. I have gone to numerous soccer, and basketball games and have seen them win quite a bit which makes me happy to see and everyone else. I hope to enjoy the rest of my freshman year and the years to come after that.

My experience at Clarke College has been nothing short of amazing. From the moment I stepped onto campus, I was welcomed into a vibrant and inclusive community. The college has provided me with numerous opportunities to connect with fellow students through a wide range of activities. Whether it's joining clubs, participating in events, or engaging in community service, I've been able to build friendships and create lasting memories. Additionally, Clarke College has done a commendable job of nurturing your spiritual growth. They have created a supportive environment where I can explore and deepen your spirituality, helping I develop a sense of purpose and meaning. Overall, my time at Clarke College has been filled with personal growth, meaningful connections, and a strong sense of community. I couldn't be happier with your college experience!

I just transfered this past semester to Clarke University from Clinton Community College. The staff at Clarke have made this transition an easy one and can't wait for my future here.
I am working in scholarships to lessen the burden. I am also in cheerleading and dance team to help with finances.
Thank you for your time!

ai_summary: Clarkson University receives mixed feedback from students regarding academics, campus life, and amenities. Many students appreciate the strong academic programs , particularly in STEM fields, and note the supportive professors who are dedicated to helping students succeed. The small class sizes foster a close-knit community where personal connections can flourish, enhancing the educational experience. However, some students mention the intense workload and rigorous curriculum can be overwhelming. In terms of student life, while there are various clubs and extracurricular activities, some students express that the campus can feel limited for social options, especially on weekends. The town of Potsdam is described as quiet with few entertainment options, although the surrounding nature offers opportunities for outdoor activities . Food quality often comes under scrutiny, although there are indications that improvements are being made. Overall, Clarkson is highlighted as a good fit for motivated students seeking strong academic and professional outcomes, especially in a collaborative and community-oriented environment.

reviews.text: Can't say enough great things about this little school. North Country is great if you love the outdoors and there are great, straightforward people who are very welcoming. Beautiful up here, and I've made great friends. Skiing at Whiteface, crossing the border to Canada for an NHL game or poutine, hiking, floatfest, and enough clubs to keep you busy if you get out of your dorm room. Teachers are great and I'm definitely prepared for my field. But like everything, any school is going to be about what you do with it. If you sit and do nothing, you get nothing. But if you are motivated and work hard, there are plenty of opportunities. You just have to get moving from Day 1. And it helps to love snow.

My four years at Clarkson were the most memorable and fun years of my life. Not only did Clarkson provide resources to exceed but the academics and career center allowed me to capitalize on my dream job after graduation. The school teaches you how to collaborate with others, time management skills, and is an incredible community to be apart of. My biggest recommendations would be to get involved in all clubs that interest you and branch out into all friend groups and activities. The people are extremely friendly, chill, and a great time. Although Potsdam is a small, rural town, it feels extremely safe and there are plenty of events if you’re looking for them. The school is 1.5 hours from lake placid and 2 hours from montreal for plenty of exploring. I highly recommend learning to ski, hiking in the adirondacks and finding hidden gems in Montreal. Con: the food isn’t the best haha.
This was the best four years of my life and now I have a successful career. Best decision I ever made.

Simply put, Clarkson's goal is providing opportunity. If you want skills, micro-credentials and the opportunity to practice working in teams through SPEED teams (robotics, Baja speed car, rocketry, etc.), Clarkson is for you. Many of my experiences never would have happened at another college. You'll be surrounded by others who also enjoy competition and hands-on learning. If you're looking for tons of entertainment, shops & restaurants like Washington Street in Hoboken, you're not going to find it here. That's not what Clarkson's about. But, if you're passion is being surrounded by focused, unpretentious students ready to engage through SPEED teams and taking advantage of career related opportunities, this place is for you. Is it cold in the winter--YES. But, for me, it's got the vibe I truly want in a college experience where I get career prepared. I'm so glad I'm here.

I am a freshman this year and it has been so easy to adjust to the new environment. All of the professors are super nice, and the peers are even better. Clarkson allows for a wide variety of bonding opportunities with first week workshops to get to meet new people and dorm RA's that create opportunities to bond with your floor mates.
I am a freshman and really have been enjoying the semester so far! It’s really easy to get involved, lots of clubs and organizations as well as help with classes.
Good + helpful professors and TA’s. Everyone is here to help. Even as a freshman there is a lot of opportunity for involvement on campus.

I am currently a freshman. So far my experience has been positive. The classes are engaging and there is a good balance of classes and free time. My professors are helpful and have been approachable and available to me. Food is fine there is plenty of variety. There isn't much to do in the surrounding area but there are plenty of clubs and organizations with a lot of activities on campus to make up for it. Overall its been a great start to my college experience.

I like how everyone here is kind, including the staff and professors. I also like how the majority of people live on campus so it really feels like a community here of all your friends and classmates.

My experience at Clarkson has been fantastic, the people I am surrounded by are awesome and supportive and the faculty and staff make a true and genuine effort to understand and help me. 100/10, would recommend!
So far my experience has been great, everyone is supper sports oriented and ready to go out and do things. It make it easy to meet new people and there are so many trips each weekend outdoors.

Only being a few weeks into my freshman year, I would say that if you are someone who is okay with being away from a city this is a great school. The job placement rate is amazing, and the alumni are great.
Great experience, I love the environment and people. Decent food, great professors, and overall a super fun time!

So I am freshman in college and have only but here for a short time but I have made so many memories and have done so many fun things along with getting adjusted to college and my new life here. One of my favorite things is all the fun activities I have done since getting here and has helped me to settle in and adjust.
i havent been here long but so far every aspect of clarkson has been amazing! everything from the professors, people, food, and atmosphere.

Clarkson University offers a solid academic environment, especially for students pursuing STEM degrees. The campus is well-equipped, with access to cutting-edge labs, research opportunities, and a focus on hands-on learning. The faculty is generally approachable, knowledgeable, and invested in student success. One of the standout features is the strong sense of community, where collaboration is encouraged.

ai_summary: Clemson University receives high marks for its strong academics and vibrant student life , characterized by a close-knit community and impressive school spirit , especially surrounding athletics. Many reviews highlight the dedication of professors and the breadth of academic resources available, although experiences may vary depending on individual faculty. The campus is noted for its beauty and safe environment, making it feel welcoming, although some students express concerns about the dining options and parking difficulties. While Clemson offers numerous opportunities for involvement through clubs and organizations, there are calls for greater diversity and improvements in student services. Overall, Clemson is perceived as an excellent choice for those seeking a spirited college experience with a balance of rigorous academics and social engagement.

reviews.text: Clemson is a family of unique individuals and staff that will do everything they can to help you have a meaningful Clemson experience. The grounds are beautiful and very walkable. There are so many clubs and events that it is hard to feel alone. Overall, I am so luck to have gotten the opportunity to spend 4 years here!
I have really enjoyed my Clemson experience so far. I feel that I am being challenged academically and am constantly being prepared for my professional career.

I went to Clemson University from 1992-1995 for my Master’s Degree in Special Education-Emotional Disorders. I absolutely loved every minute of my time in graduate school. The professors were amazing and very knowledgeable about special education IEPs, Behavior Intervention Plans, psychoeducational assessments, etc.. I could go on and on. I was completely prepared for the Praxis Exam in Emotional Disabilities which is one of the more difficult exams in Special Education. We were very informed about IDEA … it was just introduced while I was in graduate school. I most remember my Applied Behavior Analysis professor. Without that class, I would have never passed the Praxis Exam or my comprehensive exams so I could complete my degree. Because Clemson prepared me with such quality instruction and experiences, I am still a Special Education Teacher … 32 years later this year. I do not see retiring any time soon. Thank you to everyone at Clemson University who helped me become a teacher.
Overall, my experience has been good here. My intentions for transferring are solely personal, and relate to my desire for global shaped structures.
My experience is very good, Clemson university is an institution that encourages diversity and the learning environment is very much conducive. My overall experience is very good

When they talk about what it means to be in the Tiger Family, you really don’t know how true it is until you live as a Clemson student. I loved my experience at Clemson and I went there on a whim! There’s lot of clubs for students to join and a whole lot of extra resources too. The downtown is small and cute but also fun for nights out. The campus is a perfect size and you can get from one side of campus to the other in 20 minutes or less because of the way it is set up. I loved it. I would like to see the parking situation change because as of right now it is extremely hard to find student parking as a commuter.
I love Clemson. The community here is like no other. I have met so many amazing people from peers to professors, everyone here is truly amazing. The academics and athletics are great.

My overall experience at Clemson University has been positive and rewarding. I’ve appreciated the strong sense of community, supportive faculty, and the hands-on learning opportunities that make coursework feel relevant and engaging. Clemson’s emphasis on collaboration and real-world application helped me grow academically and professionally.
One thing I’ve especially liked is the access to resources, advising, and opportunities for involvement that support student success. I would like to see continued improvements in course availability and flexibility, particularly for students balancing heavy course loads, work, or other responsibilities. Overall, Clemson provided a solid educational experience and a supportive environment for growth.

Overall Clemson is an amazing accretided university, despite this there are always flaws. Being a a student for 3 years now, there are standards that I've set that have yet to been met. For instance available scholarships and guidance towards one's future path.
My experience at Clemson so far has been very amazing. The academics at the school are great and it is a prestigious school. There is a great balance between learning and fun and overall growing.
Clemson University is an amazing place to go to school! The campus is beautiful, everyone is very friendly, and there is a great campus culture! You won't go wrong if you become a Clemson Tiger!

As a freshman at Clemson University, my first semester was a wild ride. As I was trying to find myself and discover new things, however, because of how dedicated I am to my studies, I found it quite hard to attend college events or even club events. I tried partying a few times, but had to cancel many times due to studying. In addition, the nature surrounding Clemson always gave me some peace of mind. I found it quite relaxing to go on a walk when I felt low or like giving up.

I'm still only a Freshman, but I do think that this school lacks diversity. They say they're a diverse school, yet it's mostly caucasian, and their "diversity" means people from different states, but they're all the same race, so I don't know if that counts as diversity. Although the culture here is nice, the parties aren't too crazy, though either.

Clemson University is an incredible school. I am currently a sophomore, and have thoroughly enjoyed my experience thus far. As a student, you are provided with so many resources regarding academics, student life, and more. There is always someone who is willing and able to help on campus, whatever the need may be. The professors are absolutely phenomenal and are extremely passionate about their area of study. One thing that can be stressful on campus is the lack of parking for commuters. It is anxiety inducing when you give yourself extra time before class, specifically for parking, and still cannot find a spot. But, the university is actively working on solutions to this issue. Overall, Clemson is an amazing school.
Expensive for what the school provides, very hard to get course needed, also hard to find housing and parking

ai_summary: Cleveland State University (CSU) offers a diverse and supportive academic environment , with many reviewers appreciating the campus's location in downtown Cleveland, which enhances access to internships and cultural experiences. Students value the small class sizes and the generally dedicated faculty who often provide personalized support, although experiences with professors can be inconsistent. Academically, CSU is recognized for its affordability and a variety of majors, including strong nursing and film programs . While the campus is filled with student life activities and events, some feel that the commuter nature of the school limits on-campus engagement, particularly during weekends. Additionally, there are mixed feelings about student services, with requests for improved advising and career services being common, as well as concerns about campus safety and communication. Overall, many students feel that CSU is a solid choice that fosters both academic and personal growth while promoting a sense of community among its diverse student body.

reviews.text: Because CSU is located in a robust downtown Cleveland, I was able to find high paying jobs as a student. At age 20, I started investing in the stock market, and am a millionaire today because of those early investments. Thus, CSU gave me opportunities beyond just academics.
Cleveland State University is a diverse community in the heart of downtown Cleveland. Downtown Cleveland has a lot to offer and CSU professors are great.
I really like it here, I got very involved on campus and that was very easy to do. I live on campus so that definitely a positive factor opposed to commuting., with a lot of people do.

Overall the campus is pretty nice and modernized. Majority of the stem professors aren’t really that great but they do seem to have a passion for their work. The safety of the campus can be skeptical at times especially at night.

My experience at Cleveland State has been good so far. Being a part of the track team has opened lots of opportunities for me. I would say the one thing I would like to see change is the dining options.

I like the flexibility they have with their online classes & degrees. There are many different options to choose from regarding choosing majors. I would like to to see more scholarships offered by the college.

As a commuter student at Cleveland State University, my experience differs from that of students who live on campus. Overall, I have enjoyed my time at CSU, and there are several positive aspects worth highlighting. To start, the university offers a wide range of resources that are extremely helpful and easily accessible. CSU is also very well organized, which has made it easy for me to navigate academic expectations and campus procedures. Additionally, the university is highly diverse, both in its student body and faculty. This helps create an inclusive learning environment.
One challenge I have encountered is the limited parking spaces. This results in higher-priced parking passes and, at times, competition for parking spaces. Parking is the primary issue I would like to see improve. Overall, CSU is a strong university; however, its urban location can present challenges related to parking and, occasionally, safety.

Cleveland State University is a great school because it offers strong academic programs, dedicated professors, and a diverse campus that supports students from many different backgrounds. CSU provides many opportunities for growth and success, one area that could improve is the parking pass system. Parking passes are often expensive and limited, which can add unnecessary stress for commuting students who already balance school, work, and other responsibilities.

I like being at Cleveland State University. But it does have its issues. The dining hall area doesn't have enough different food choices and it's always the same thing every day. Although they do some days have different things, it's usually the same as before. Some of the events aren't as entertaining as much, but I still have fun at the events. I'm met new people through those events. When I first came to CSU, there was this event that they do the week before classes, and I went to it with my roommates at the time. They explained the history of CSU and the resources you could find if you needed. After that, they had a kind of street fair kind of thing that went on and that's where I met one of my now friends who at the time was also a film major like me. She now does graphic design. My film teachers are amazing. They make the class fun and exciting. There's barely a dull moment in class with those teachers. The classes are a little hard at times, but I always try pushing through it.

Overall, Cleveland State is a very nice college for people who are interested in a school with solid academics and campus life but with a more affordable price. The facilities were nice, the food was decent, and the dorms were incredibly beautiful and way above par. I'd like to see more student engagement in campus life and more organizations that promote fun events, a campus community, and getting students out of their comfort zones. I'd also like to see certain programs (especially the Film and Media Arts program I'm in) focus more heavily on getting their students networking and experiential opportunities. But if you're looking for a school that packs solid stats without the price, CSU is the place for you.

Cleveland State University has been a great experience so far. The campus feels welcoming, the staff are helpful, and the resources for students make the transition easier. I appreciate the flexible class options and the supportive environment overall

Cleveland State is on a decline despite steep costs. There is little in the way of useful supports for students and student led organizations are hidden and denied their agreed on funding. Additionally STEM labs are poorly stocked leading to broken equipment being shared across lab groups. Even the list of avaliable courses has been shrinking as higher ups boot planning, historical, political, and ethical disciplines. The professors I have met have been excellent and passionate but everyone above them might as well be theives looking to scam students out of a quality university experience.

I wouldn't recommend the Nursing Program to anyone and how this establishment is still granted accreditation is something to ponder about. Leadership is disorganized and cannot communicate effectively on important matters. So-called "professors" do not care about educating the next generation of nurses and are only here to collect a paycheck by doing the bare minimum, which includes insulting students if they don't understand the material instead of helping them understand not only for the NCLEX, but for the field itself. Dates and times are always changed at late notice as well. The entire program needs a overhaul and those who display gross acts of incivility need to be fired or given disciplinary action. Instructors should not harass students based on their background, endanger their lives, or blame the mistakes of others on them in clinical settings. For a university that has a reputed nursing program, this is beyond embarrassing and they need to get their act together.

Cleveland State University is a great school for commuting in my opinion, because it is close and I think it is not a party college, and more so a safe option for most students. More so, the campus is nice and there are a lot of great opportunities here.
My experience has been good for the most part, except for one of my classes I have a professor with a heavy accent which at times can be difficult to understand what he is saying.

ai_summary: At Colorado School of Mines, the academic environment is characterized by its rigorous and demanding coursework typical of a STEM-focused institution, with many students highlighting the dedication and support from professors. There’s a strong emphasis on hands-on learning and real-world applications, which prepares students well for their future careers, especially in engineering. However, this academic intensity leaves less time for recreational activities, and students note a limited social scene compared to larger universities. While the campus and surrounding area in Golden offer beautiful outdoor opportunities , there are mixed feelings about the dining options and campus diversity. Most students appreciate the close-knit community and support systems available, helping to foster collaboration among peers, though some express frustrations with aspects of campus life such as the quality of dorms and administrative processes. Overall, students find value in the education provided and the opportunities for professional growth.

reviews.text: I like the environment and the professors - I have never felt like a nerd. There are times where I've had people look down on me because of my gender, but most people are very accepting and just want to have a conversation about STEM.

A lot of the people who go to this school have a lot in common, yet there are a lot of diverse interests despite this. The housing is great, the food is hated on a little bit too much, and the education/opportunities really make Mines shine.

A parent. The school definitely is a tough school. Not for the faint mind. Engineering school. Need get problem solved. Producing quality kids who can work. Recognized by other STEM school. They are proud of their nerdy images around and say what they can do. Lots of late night work to be done if you want to have good grades. No kidding around. Students are super friendly to each other.
Just about the best school I could’ve asked for going into such a rough job market. Tough but super rewarding. You get out what you put in, go talk to people and make friends!

Mines is a great school for engineering and has top professors. It is a research university and that is prioritized over other things. If you are sure about engineering it is awesome, but there are limited opportunities to explore non-engineering related majors
It is a good school. If you join clubs and get involved with the school, it is easy to find your people. It is academically challenging but rewarding.

Colorado school of mines is a very stunningly beautiful and diverse college. It has a lot of amazing professors and people who can help you elevate your college experience, and prepare you for the future.

Mines is great. The community is amazing. The academics are challenging, but exciting and fulfilling. I have so many opportunities to learn and get involved in really great things like research, careers, assistance from professionals in engineering fields, clubs that are focused on interesting science topics, conferences, honors programs, and much more. The community is also really great, everyone works together and helps lift each other up through this part of our lives, even when it is challenging.

Overall, I has been really nice. The food is typical college but the stir fry is nice and there are plently of meal exchange options. There is a small party scene but you have to be invited but, just show up to the parties and eventually you will more invites. Lots of studying. Like the study spots are numerous but typically full. Most of my teachers are great however, there is 1-2 that just read off slides. There is a lot of school spirt and the merch is quite cute. Overall, lots of walking uphill, both physically due to being at the base of the rockies and mentally from the STEM classes.
For my first year I am amazed by the diversity of the people I meet. I have been welcomed by many clubs and can be as involved as I want to be. I like being around people with similar goals.
Mines is a wonderful school and the student outcomes are amazing. I have never seen so many people have internships and jobs lined up before they graduate.

Overall, my experience has been extremely beneficial to myself. While some aspects like the food are not great, I have foster a great social life like I never have before, have access to lots of resources for my major, and resources to help myself if I ever struggle.

My overall experience with Mines has been amazing. The professors have been invested in their classes and assisting students. The dorms have different rooms with "themes" (i.e. music, quiet, study,). Mines is a D2 school when coming to athletics. Downtown Golden has so much to offer, Colorado trails are amazing, and the Red Rocks Amphitheater are great spots to go off campus.
The professors, especially within the math department are amazing. Parking is awful but they’re improving with new parking garages. There’s not much of a party scene except once a year for E-Days.

My experience at Colorado School of Mines has been both challenging and rewarding. The academic environment is rigorous and pushes you to grow, but the professors are passionate and supportive. It’s a place where hard work is expected, but you’re surrounded by a community that wants to see you succeed.

ai_summary: At Colorado State University, students often describe the campus as beautiful and welcoming, with a strong sense of community and numerous opportunities to engage both academically and socially. Reviews highlight a vibrant, active atmosphere and a range of clubs and activities that foster inclusivity, although some students note a lack of diversity on campus. Academics receive mixed feedback; while many praise passionate professors and supportive staff, others express concerns about inconsistent teaching quality and administrative challenges within certain departments. Student life reveals that while CSU offers a limited nightlife , its sports events and outdoor activities are popular. Dining experiences are varied, with some enjoying the options, while others cite dissatisfaction with food quality. Many students appreciate the strong resources available for academic and personal support, positioning CSU as a viable choice for a well-rounded college experience.

reviews.text: The staff is supportive and informative. I enjoy the tools and accommodations that the university has provided to help me succeed in furthering my education. Living in Colorado you usually have to interact with a predominantly white community. I have noticed more diversity and I can appreciate that as well.
My overall experience with CSU has been good. The profressors are great, they have lots of help available. The resources are endless. Everyone is friendsly and including.
CSU is a well rounded experience. The campus is the perfect size for a college and the students are friendly.

I have enjoyed my first year on campus. My professors have been great, the campus is nice, and there are a lot of opportunities to get involved and meet other students. The dorms could use some updating though!

Colorado State University is amazing, the community is so welcoming you can meet a new friend almost anywhere. There’s a lot of clubs to join to, classes aren’t bad workload is good and there’s a lot of resources that you can use if your struggling.
really enjoying parmalee hall and all the friends I've made so far. The dining hall at this dorm is great, and the rec center is close by. Get involved, it will be worth it!
I really enjoy how environmentally focused the school is. I also really enjoy most of my classes. It can be hard to socialize if you're more of an introvert.
Campus is nice and there are a lot of opportunities for community for all kinds of interests. Campus food was pretty good, and students are generally open to talking to new people.
Amazing school, and there really isn't anything else to say about it. Everyone was amazing, and I plan on possibly doing my graduate schooling there.
I am just a first-year student here still! But I have truly been enjoying my time, and I enjoy all things CSU has to offer.

Colorado State University in Fort Collins generally receives positive reviews for its beautiful, walkable campus, strong sense of community, and excellent, supportive faculty. Students praise the balance between academics and outdoor life in a friendly,, energetic town. However, some reviewers note limited diversity, higher costs, and mixed experiences with dorm quality.

I love Colorado State University; the overall environment is welcoming, and most teachers that I've had are very understanding and accommodating. There are so many resources. If you have a question or you are confused about an aspect of your college life, there will almost always be someone there to help; you just have to ask!

Overall, CSU was a good choice for me because it allowed me to meet people from different areas without feeling isolated. Where they lack is in some teaching styles that are not executed well, and depending on the teacher or TA you get, it can change your class experience. That said, if I needed to change anything, I would hope one day all student housing had AC in the dorms, not just in one or two. I was lucky enough to have the AC, but I know for those who did not, nighttime could be a struggle in the spring semester / on hot days.
CSU has exceeded my expectations of an online college experience. It is an honor to learn from the professors there. The foundation of respect and inclusion is very welcoming.

I attended in-person for three years to earn my BS in Psychology. I attended online for a year and a half towards a Masters. I have nothing bad to say about anything. The football team could be better and looks to be on the right track toward that goal.

ai_summary: At Colorado Technical University - Colorado Springs, students consistently highlight the supportive faculty and dedicated staff who go out of their way to help them succeed in their online courses. Many reviews mention how easy it is to navigate the learning platform, and the flexibility it provides allows students to balance their studies with personal and professional responsibilities. However, some concerns were raised about technical issues with certain programs and the associated costs of tuition. Overall, the university is seen as a good choice for those seeking a supportive environment for their education, with an emphasis on practical learning and career advancement opportunities . The sense of community, even in an online format, alongside resources like success coaches, contributes to a positive student experience .

reviews.text:
Oneida, KY is truly beautiful. The wildlife, the mountains, and the way families look out for each other give this place a strong sense of home. I’d love to see more jobs come into the area and maybe a new park or something for the kids to do, because they deserve more opportunities and safe spaces to enjoy.
After ten years of waiting, something finally shifted. I got a call from an adviser named Kelly. I had been looking at schools, but honestly, I wasn’t really feeling it. That one conversation changed the entire direction of my life. She helped me see that I could better myself, that it wasn’t too late, i deserved the chance to grow an dre
Now I’m working toward my Bachelor of Science in Criminal Justice, and I’m proud of the path I’m on. One of my biggest goals is to create an outreach program to help people in my community — the same community that raised me, challenged me, and shaped me. I want to be part of the change that gives people hope, support, and a chance to rebuild.

My overall experience with Colorado Technical University – Colorado Springs has been very positive. I appreciated the flexibility of the programs, which made it possible to balance school with work and personal responsibilities. The instructors were knowledgeable, supportive, and brought real-world experience into the classroom, which made the coursework practical and engaging. The online learning platform was easy to navigate, and the academic support services were helpful whenever I needed guidance. Overall, CTU provided a supportive learning environment that helped me stay motivated and confident in reaching my educational goals.

My overall experience with Colorado Technical University has been positive. What stands out the most is how flexible and structured the online format is, which makes it easier to balance school with work and personal responsibilities.
The courses are organized clearly, and the expectations for assignments and discussion boards are straightforward. I also appreciate that there are resources available when questions come up, especially for students returning to school or adjusting to online learning.
CTU works well for motivated students who are willing to stay on top of deadlines and manage their time. The program encourages consistency, accountability, and personal responsibility, which has helped me build better habits as a student.
Overall, CTU has been a solid choice for me, and I would recommend it to anyone looking for a flexible online education that fits into a busy life.

ai_summary: Columbia University provides a rigorous academic environment that encourages personal and intellectual growth, with many reviews highlighting the school's distinguished faculty and diverse student body . The Core Curriculum is a notable feature, fostering critical thinking through a broad range of subjects, though opinions on its relevance vary. While the location in New York City is often seen as a significant advantage—offering access to cultural experiences and networking opportunities—some students feel that the campus atmosphere can feel isolated from the city and overly competitive among peers. Reviews also express concerns about the administration’s support for student services, particularly for those needing mental health resources. Despite high tuition fees, many students find the educational and extracurricular opportunities worthwhile, contributing to a vibrant community , though they also mention the importance of finding supportive groups within this large institution.

reviews.text: Said they lost my sensitive information. Never even applied here. Unhelpful with any questions about this. This situation alone illustrates lack of ethics, responsibility, and competence.

My overall experience with Columbia University has been shaped by learning about its culture, academic intensity, and the kind of people it attracts. From everything I’ve explored, Columbia stands out as a place where curiosity is taken seriously. . I like the idea of sitting in a small class discussing works that have shaped history, politics, philosophy, and literature, then stepping outside into New York City and seeing how those ideas play out in real life.
What makes Columbia compelling to me is the balance between tradition and urgency. As an Ivy League institution, it carries a long academic legacy, yet it feels deeply connected to the present. Being located in Manhattan means students are not isolated in a campus bubble. Internships, research opportunities, cultural events, and global conversations are woven into everyday life. I value that kind of exposure because growth often happens when you are stretched beyond the classroom.

My experience with Columbia University has been academically rewarding and intellectually challenging. The faculty are knowledgeable, the coursework is rigorous, and the university’s location in New York City offers exceptional exposure to industry and networking opportunities.
I especially valued the diverse classroom perspectives and strong academic resources. However, administrative processes could be more streamlined, and greater clarity around fees and funding would improve the overall experience.
Overall, Columbia provides a strong learning environment for motivated students who make full use of its resources.
I liked all about it, but it was stressful on campus with balancing education and working experience

Faculty goes above and beyond to ensure that students have a multifaceted understanding of diverse topics. Student body is incredibly diverse and everyone I've met has positively contributed to my experience.

Columbia is a wonderful school for driven students. The academics can be demanding/rigorous, but the school is really supportive of its students. The location, New York City, is unmatched for career opportunities (finance, consulting, and law are typical career paths for students), outings with friends, and learning how to "adult." Columbia's campus is secure, and I've never felt unsafe wandering around campus. That said, Columbia's fast-paced, competitive culture and NYC can be a bit overwhelming for some people. The Core Curriculum that all students in the College are required to take is cool, but it can give you less freedom to explore fields of study. I'm in the College, but my friends in the School of Engineering and Applied Science (SEAS) recommend it (even though most people don't associate Columbia with STEM). The biggest complaint students have, other than the Core, is the lack of campus culture.
Love that it is in New York City. Additionally, it is inspiring to be surrounded by amazing scholars who are leading some world-class research labs.

My intellectual growth and professional development at Columbia was both very vigorous and grew from an intellectual base. I am thankful for the ability to push myself academically in conjunction with having world-class faculty members. I was able to get conducting serious research and scholarship participation early in my experience. In addition, having New York City as my environment not only gives my time there many options outside the walls of the university, it also has opened doors for me to have unique opportunities to learn about different communities and cultures, as well as provide me opportunities for internships and jobs, and help broaden my worldview. However, I have also felt the pressure of the high-stress nature of the environment, as well as having difficulty navigating the administrative processes of the university, particularly in terms of receiving and accessing resources for academic advising, funding sources, and mental health support.

Attending Columbia has been one of the most transformative opportunities of my life. Coming in as a nontraditional student, I was not sure what to expect, but I found professors who are not only incredibly knowledgeable, but genuinely warm and welcoming. They challenge us with intention. They create an environment where you are stretched, pushed, and sharpened, not for the sake of difficulty, but for the sake of growth.
Being here means engaging with a rigorous curriculum and a community that expects excellence, and it is an honor to rise to that standard every day. I truly love my school. My only hope is that Columbia continues expanding opportunities and funding for nontraditional students like me, because we bring a unique strength, perspective, and resilience that enrich the Columbia community.

This school is absolutely amazing. If accepted you win twice; a top school in America's top city. This school is unmatched in academics, student body, and student life. Probably the best all-around school in America. Top departments in every subject. Endless course catalogue. The campus is beautiful, safe, and the classes are great. No fluff classes here to fill requirements, all academic. The Core classes are probably the best classes a student would ever take anywhere.

It is the most amazing universty i've been so far
i studied Politics there and it was Holy
Like as i gifted student its a pleasure to meet the professors and the Staff who support me
Thanks Columbia university for making my dream come true
Only been here a month but very good first impressions overall, owing to the impeccable dining, supportive professors, and safe campus.

As a transfer student, I can say that Columbia is a big school in a big city. All the professors I had the opportunity to interact seem accesible and patient. Although in the community college I was before all professors were closed to their students, here at Columbia, most of the professors are opened to listen and talk to every student.
