Overall, my experience has helped me grow academically and adjust to college life.

I honestly do like attending Indiana State University, it suits me quite nicely. I prefer the campus life I have here over other colleges where I believe I would feel overwhelmed. I came here because not only was it close to where I was already living, but it also has a good reputation in psychology. I took advantage of this and am currently attending as an undergrad majoring in psychology.

So far I like the overall feel of the campus. It is a smaller campus so it feel like a tight community. Your classes are small so you can build great connections with your peers around you. You can tell that there is a lot of sycamore pride in the community.

After spending four years at Indiana State University, I couldn't have asked for a more well-rounded undergraduate experience. I was able to participate in athletics, serving as the university mascot for three years. I completed two years of undergraduate research that led me to my dream job directly out of college. I participated in Greek Life as a member of Gamma Phi Beta, where I made lifelong friends and sisters and a support system that I will carry forever.
One thing that sticks out to me about Indiana State University is the accommodating nature of the university. As someone who suffers from many food allergies, I had many options and was never without access to safe and healthy options. I also suffer from a chronic illness, for which I was never punished and was able to complete my work and demonstrate my competence in the area in a way that worked for me and worked around doctor visits and sick days.

⭐ A supportive vibe:
Faculty and staff tend to be approachable. You’re not just lost in a crowd. If you put in effort, people will meet you halfway.
⭐ Strong programs (especially in education, nursing, business, and criminal justice):
These areas get a lot of praise for hands-on learning and good career support.
⭐ Campus life that’s what you make it:
There are plenty of organizations, events, and community stuff — but ISU isn’t a wild party school. It’s more “chill hangout with your people” than “never sleep again.”
⭐ Terre Haute life:
Listen… it’s not a flashy city. But cost of living is low, things are easy to navigate, and students usually say it grows on them. Think cozy, not glamorous.
⭐ Good value:
ISU tends to be affordable and offers strong financial aid and scholarship options, especially for Indiana residents
I have family member who have graduated from Indiana State University. I love the campus and how friendly everyone is. I'm looking forward to attending ISU fall of 2026.

Pilot in flight program. Airport close by and planes readily available with great instructors. Flight plans on weekends and evenings allow time for study and social life. sorority life is very special and classes allow me to go home often even though the ride is 2 hours

I've had a good experience at Indiana State University, and I think others can feel at home here. The classes are good and all the professors I've had really care about your success. Especially if you're an art student, there's a strong sense of community among the students and professors. The people on campus in general are very friendly, and I've always felt safe on campus. My only complaint is that I looked into Indiana State University as a LGBTQ+ friendly college, and that was very much true my first year. Since then, they've done away with rules and events that made me feel so secure last year. Everyone is still accepting, and I've even been able to use my preferred name in the systems for the college, but events such as the Terre Haute Pride festival are no longer on campus. The school has also removed the Latinx and Gender Inclusive Living-Learning communities. Despite these issues, I've had an overall positive experience with the college and recommend it to all!
I'm online/distance learning student. It is great even been online to communicate with the instructors and have them respond promptly, which is very helpful.

My overall experience with Indiana State University has been both supportive and transformative. From the start, the faculty and staff created an environment where I felt encouraged to challenge myself academically while also growing as an individual. The programs are well-structured, offering a balance of rigorous coursework and practical experiences that help connect theory to real-world applications.
I always felt that my education was a shared priority. Indiana State also emphasizes diversity, inclusion, and innovation, which has broadened my perspective.
Overall, my time at Indiana State University has given me both the knowledge and the confidence to pursue my professional goals while fostering personal growth.
There’s nothing that Indiana State needs to change.
Love the campus, nice and clean dorms, one on one help with professors if needed, diversity on campus
The professors were not very cooperative. I have had a different advisor every year, and out of the 4 or 5 I have had, only one has actually helped me with my issues.

My experience at Indiana State University has been wonderful so far. The entire Nursing Education staff has been so welcoming and helpful. I have enjoyed attending this college more than others I have attended previously.
Making college more affordable for middle class students who are paying for college without any financial support from parents.

ai_summary: At Indiana University Indianapolis, students appreciate the vibrant urban campus that offers a multitude of activities, clubs, and career opportunities due to its downtown location. Many reviews highlight the supportive professors and the wealth of academic resources available, such as tutoring centers and specialized programs, which foster a strong learning environment . However, the commuter nature of the school means that student life can feel less engaging, particularly on weekends, as many students go home after classes. Safety concerns, especially related to the surrounding city environment, are frequently mentioned, alongside frustrations about parking costs and limited dining options. The diversity among the student body and a strong emphasis on inclusivity are seen as positives, making it an attractive choice for students seeking both a quality education and a sense of community amidst a bustling city backdrop.

reviews.text: I enjoyed my time at IU Indianapolis. I learned valuable skills that prepared me for work force. I was very active on campus and created a lot of student organizations that helped me meet people and network with the community. The faculty and staff were amazing.

I believe that Indiana University Indianapolis is a really great school to attend. This college is not too big and not too small, so you get the feel of a big campus in some aspects and the feel of a small campus in some aspects. I am specifically here for the healthcare field and IU Indianapolis is a fantastic school to go into healthcare and I would highly recommend it for that.

It’s a pretty nice school. The area is in Indy, so you can’t expect more than what the city provides there. They have a very vast amount of programs. I really love the art school there, Herron, specifically. I think in that area is much better than Bloomington or any other schools nearby really. You have to accustom to the student life there a bit but people are very kind, and I’ve had no issues at all. I’d maybe like the area to looks a bit prettier maybe? For as big of a school as IUI is, you would think they’d have more artsy architecture and artworks around.

My experience during my first year at IUI has been excellent. The school is an easy commute, has a welcoming layout and the educators seem to want every student to succeed. There are numerous opportunities for athletics on multiple levels and also special interest activities.
My experience with housing and dining would be the two areas I think need improvement. But there are also many alternatives to both.

As a first-generation college freshman, I expected my experience at IUI to be much more intimidating than it actually was. This campus has been so easy to get around and integrate into. Out of everything, I enjoy the people and the campus itself the most. Almost everyone I've met at IUI has been extremely kind and helpful; I've had almost zero bad experiences with anyone here. The campus itself is gorgeous throughout the seasons and is very accessible to all sorts of students. The tunnels provide a safe, warm way to navigate through campus in the dark winters, and the greenery can be beautiful to be around in the summer. If I had to change anything, I would change the issues with heating in the buildings. Occasionally, there are issues where the buildings are either too hot or too cold to sit in, but they usually get managed very quickly. Overall, this was the best college choice for me, and I am beyond grateful that I chose to come here.

I really enjoy being a student at Indiana University Indianapolis. I have made great friendships and connections that will allow me a head start into the industry. I love being involved in student organizations on campus that feel like home.

The majority of available social events take place during bad times for me as a working student. The only friends I made were friends of other transfer students. More social events, especially club meetings, should take place on weekends so that working students like myself can attend.

I transferred into Indiana University Indianapolis this year from Northern Michigan University. While the scenery at Northern Michigan University was beautiful (the northern lights), being in a city has its perks. I have my own apartment and am centrally located to do activities in the city. I enjoy going to the university's gym for working out. The classes are interesting. Attending this school is far more challenging based on the prestige and curriculum. Additionally, I have played various sports and having our football team undefeated this year is just the cherry on top.

So far, I'm liking my experience with IUI. I like the diversity. Being a transfer student and coming from a predominantly white college, it's nice seeing the diversity on campus. Even though we're just starting the semester, I do like my teachers here at the Herron School of Art & Design. They are willing to teach you, but also be there for you if you need help. Everyone here has been helpful and accommodating to my new experience being here at IUI. I would like to see a change in the number of majors they have. There used to be more majors now there aren't that many due to the split from Purdue University. Other than that, it's a great school to start your college experience with.

My experience at Indiana University Indianapolis has been incredibly positive and rewarding. From the start, I felt welcomed by the campus environment and the sense of community it offers. I quickly met a great group of friends who share similar goals and values, and we have remained close, supporting each other both academically and personally. I have also built strong connections with my professors, who are approachable, encouraging, and genuinely invested in student success. I enjoy my classes and appreciate how engaging and applicable the material is. IU Indianapolis feels like a place where I belong and continue to grow.

Overall, I love my college. And I am grateful to be here to have a chance to pursue my studying and making my dream, my goals become true. This is a safety place for me to go to study, the professors and faculty staffs are all respect me, my ideas and my questions. They are also willing to help whenever I have questions. The only one thing I am worry is the financial burden, I am immigrant and I live in US by myself, I am really need help with the tuition and expenses. I am trying to apply as much as scholarships as I can. Because I still keep go to school and earn the degree.

There is quite a few things about this university that I enjoy, but my favorite is the way this campus is set up. It has quick and easy access to pretty much all the academic buildings because it is connected throughout the campus by overhead tunnels. I am super happy to not have to worry about traffic and crosswalks. In the winter you can stay toasty and in summer you don't have to walk into class all sweaty and hot. The food selection throughout the campus is just right, but I love the fact that if wanted, I could order doordash for certain cravings, and have it delivered to where I am on campus. Maneuvering through city is easy whether it is on foot, scooter or in my car. I commute to school from Avon so going to school or getting back home after is super easy. All of the Administrators, student workers, staff, as well as the teachers I have encountered have all been very helpful and know ledged in what they do. The application and onboarding process was stress free.

I would let other students know that if they want the most out of their college experience, that they should go somewhere else. IU Indianapolis is basically just school, its not like a fun campus to be on, but it does help you prepare for the real world.
I’m not currently in college but I been there a lot, tour or anything I needed they helped me and be there for me

My experience at Indiana University Indianapolis has been pretty good overall. I really like the diverse environment and how supportive most professors are toward students. The campus feels welcoming, and there are plenty of resources to help with academics and mental health. I don’t really have any suggestions for changes, it’s been a positive experience so far.

ai_summary: Indiana University of Pennsylvania offers a vibrant campus experience , highlighted by a beautiful environment and a strong sense of community. Many students appreciate the dedication of professors, who are described as caring and supportive, particularly in helping students navigate their academic paths and fostering personal connections. However, the dining options on campus are a common point of critique, with several reviewers citing a need for greater variety and improved hours . While social life at IUP includes a notable party scene , there are also numerous opportunities for extracurricular involvement, clubs, and networking, which many students find enriching. The campus size is seen as manageable, allowing for easy navigation and personal interactions. Overall, IUP's blend of academic support, campus activities, and a welcoming atmosphere makes it a solid choice for a range of students, though some challenges remain regarding food quality and certain academic experiences.

reviews.text:
Smaller classes
A traditional campus environment
Strong programs in specific fields like business, education, or music.
I need in I up since fall semester, I’m on my 2nd semester. So far my experience has been good in campus.

Indiana University of Pennsylvania is an amazing school! I felt welcomed by my professors aswell as other students. The academics are great, housing is taken care of very well, and the food is also amazing.

It is a pretty good campus. There are tons of student organizations and activities. Small class sizes, which is nice. There are some party people; however, if you don't party, there are activities and people on campus that will work for you. Depending on the department, the fault is nice and will help.
I joined IUP as a dual enrollment student in the summer of 2025 and since then my experience there has been absolutely amazing .

IUP is in a small town in a rural county in PA. I am a student athlete and being in a small town helps keep my focus. There are a lot of student activities to get involved in and you do not have the sprawl of a large campus. The professors are very helpful. I did have a medical emergency last spring and the Administration was very understanding. They have been helping me get back on track after returning this fall.

My experience at Indiana University of Pennsylvania has been great for the most part. I have done well academically, translating to a GPA of 3.19 currently. I have made a ton of life-long friends, as well as, connections to reach out to for the rest of my life. I have also learned many skills to take with me into the future, too. These skills including knowledge surrounding nutrition, time management, discipline, people skills, and many more. I feel that IUP has done well, so far, in setting me up for the next step.

I love the atmosphere of the campus. I enjoy that it is a walking campus which ensures student safety and physical activity. The staff are present and helpful and the professor seek to gave each student strive based on their capabilities. Overall I think IUP is an amazing campus. I do think online programmed courses should be removed when paying for an in person class. That is my only critique.
Indiana university is a very beautiful campus! Everyone here is very helpful! Indiana wants you to succeed at everything. They are very respectful and caring for student life!

I liked going to a new place and being able to learn new things, and It is nice that I can keep doing what I like and being with other people who are so nice and also plan to go in the culinary field.
I love how welcoming the campus students and staff are. The transition from high school to college was fairly easy due to the supports the college has.
It has been a very interesting experience. I have only spent two years here being a transfer student, however, there have been ups and downs during my time here.
The campus is absolutely beautiful and it felt like home. There was a good balance of academics and social life. Staff was friendly and accomidating.

Part of my family has lived in Indiana for years, I've always been around the town and school and I grew to love it. I especially love the size of the campus and how close it is to home, I toured many colleges but IUP was always my first choice. The atmosphere really makes me feel welcomed and comfortable, as a transfer student it could feel awkward coming to a new school but I don't get that feeling with IUP.
I am just entering the college as a freshman...I have visited it several times...loved what I saw...met my professor...loved her...I'm sure this is going to be an amazing experience for me.

ai_summary: Indiana University - Bloomington is known for its beautiful campus and welcoming atmosphere , making it an enjoyable place for students to form friendships and create lasting memories. Academically, the university offers a wide variety of programs, particularly noted for strong departments like the Kelley School of Business, though some students mention that large class sizes can affect engagement. Faculty generally receive positive feedback for their support and dedication, with many professors being praised for their approachability. Student life is vibrant, featuring numerous activities and organizations, although there are mixed feelings about the party culture and Greek life, which some students feel is overwhelming. While the campus is seen as diverse and inclusive , there are concerns about campus safety and mental health support. Reviews also indicate that navigating the large campus can be challenging, especially in inclement weather, and some students are disappointed by the dorm conditions. Overall, IU Bloomington offers a unique blend of academic rigor, social opportunities, and a close-knit community feel despite its size.

reviews.text: I took a tour there on March 10th and I had an amazing experience. The campus is beautiful and the enviornment is everything I wanted. It was accepting and divorce; one of the main reasons I want to go there. The local area of Bloomington is just amazing to walk around, see all the things to do and I'm excited to go out with friends and eat local food and go to local events. The academic portion also is incredible. The amount of classes make me excited to go, because there are so many classes on things I'm interested in: such as a philosophy class on existentialism and marxism; both classes I can take at Indiana University Bloomington.
The ambiance in IU Bloomington is very nice, it's easy to make friends, the courses are very interesting and the university helps me to picture my future
Amazing community and great big campus that still gives a small-town feel. The academics were wonderful.
So far, I've loved my experience at Indiana University. I've been able to meet and make a lot of new people. And I'm very grateful for the opportunity to come here.

My experience at Indiana University Bloomington has been both challenging and rewarding. The university has pushed me to grow academically while giving me access to perspectives, strong faculty support, and meaningful opportunities to explore my interests. Being part of such a collaborative and driven community has helped me develop critical thinking skills and a clearer sense of my goals.
So far my experience here has been very good. My teachers are nice and there's a lot to do in Bloomington, you never get bored.

I couldn't have asked for a better college experience. When I think of IU I think of one word : home. I was so thankful for all of the club opportunities. I was able to try new things, and meet people of all different backgrounds. I genuinely see the world differently than I did before IU. Through the courses I took with amazing professors, I am forever changed. Additionally, I tried new things at school I never thought I would! I went to IU not liking sports and hating the idea of greek life. I ended being someone who got season tickets for basketball and football my sophmore and senior years, and I rushed a sorority! I made lifelong friends, and met the person who I believe I will marry. IU is also a STUNNING campus. I love all of the old architecture : limestone buildings, carved sculptures into the buildings, fountains, wooden floors, and clocks throughout campus. I was a club leader, an RA and I couldn't imagine doing all of those things anywhere else. I blossomed in college!

My experience at Indiana University has truly been one to remember. As a current freshman, I was initially hesitant about adjusting to college life, even though I came from a large high school. The idea of moving away from home felt overwhelming, and I wasn’t sure how long it would take for IU to feel familiar. However, I have never felt so welcomed.
I’ve met so many people through classes, social life, and campus events. Those interactions have helped me build meaningful friendships that provide a strong support system. IU’s community is one of my favorite parts of being here—there is a genuine sense of openness and belonging that makes it easy to connect with others, even in such a large school.

Indiana University in Bloomington Indiana is the most beautiful college campus I have ever seen. Growing up I had the opportunity to visit the campus many times since my dad, brother, grandparents are alumni we found reasons when we were in the area. At first the campus appeared very big and felt overwhelming to think of being able to make it to class. But the reality is that it is perfect. I attend the university now and cannot be happier with my choice for college. I live in a dorm as a freshman and the experience is good. I have been able to meet and make new friends that I would not have in a different setting. The best thing about the dorm is actually the food, it is very good. I cannot complain about any of my professors, there was a big adjustment on my part from high school to college classes and learning how to manage your day with classes spread out. What I love the most about IU is the true college experience for college sports and activities, I love everything about IU.

Indiana University - Bloomington has an amazing International Studies program to support study abroad students, efforts, and international connections which was my personal favorite part of the school. I really enjoyed all of the classes in the Media school and art school as well. Kelley business school is what IUB is known for and while it is certainly a quality education, many of the classes are “weed-out” and you don’t learn things related to your major until your last year of your undergraduate degree. I think that could stand to change and also some of the Kelley teachers are successful in their profession but not cut out for teaching others how to acquire those skills. This all being said the people you meet are lifetime friends, and really hard workers usually. The campus is beautiful, and there is a lot of opportunities to try new things through clubs. All in all the staff outside of Kelley are the best teachers I have encountered as well.

My experience at Indiana University Bloomington has been eye opening to say the least. From the beginning this place has felt like home and the transition from high school to college has been fairly easy for me. The college scenery is beautiful and I love seeing the campus change as the season goes by. Our teams are doing great and statistically this is the best our football team has ever been and I am proud to say I am a part of it. In my opinion this has been a great first year and I genuinely couldn’t name a thing I would change about it!

My experience in the Special Education transition-to-teaching program has been marked by inconsistent communication, limited instructor availability unless I initiate contact, and coursework that does not reflect real-world practice. Instructors often make assumptions about incomplete work without seeking context and give feedback that does not address the assignment requirements. As a student with documented accommodations, support is inconsistent. Any discussion of required adjustments must always be initiated by me, and recommended options for managing workload are not supported. Advising functions the same way—no proactive outreach or regular check-ins. Only one instructor has ever reached out to clarify concerns, and this was described as outside program expectations. Overall, the structure is not accessible for graduate students balancing full-time work and documented academic needs.

I am currently a freshman here at IU, and I have had quite the experience so far. They say college is all about self-discovery, and I can attest to that. I started this year as a pre-business exploratory major, but I changed it within the first week of classes. The environment immediately threw me off. I just wasn't into it, and that's okay. Now, my current goal is to take different classes and meet with career coaches to develop a further understanding of the major I would like to pursue.

Coming to Bloomington was one of the best decisions I ever made. I met so many diverse people from all sorts of backgrounds, ate a variety of different cuisines (Korean food is by far my favorite), and have received so many opportunities to further develop my undergrad education experience.
I have really enjoyed everything at Indiana University, from the dining hall and food options, to the engagement in student life.

ai_summary: Iowa State University (ISU) offers a vibrant community atmosphere where students often find strong friendships and a sense of belonging, especially through residence halls and various student organizations. The campus is noted for its beauty and accessibility, contributing to an inviting environment . Academically, ISU is recognized for its dedicated professors and diverse majors , particularly in agriculture, engineering, and business-related fields. Many students express satisfaction with the academic support services available, such as tutoring and career services, although some highlight concerns regarding dining options and financial aid processes. While the school is bustling with activities, including Greek life and sports, some reviewers mention challenges related to campus diversity and accessibility, particularly for out-of-state students. Overall, the experiences shared reflect a balanced mix of academic and social opportunities, making ISU a strong candidate for those seeking a comprehensive college experience.

reviews.text: The entire university has been pleasant and welcoming. From day one of applying to moving and playing hockey for the university and can’t imagine them doing much to make my experience anymore successful. I am being asked by staff how things are going they always go out of the way to assist in anything that I need.
I like it, good people, dorms have cliques, professors don't make sense for engineering classes, but other than that, it's a good school.

My experience at Iowa State has been extremely eye opening. I transferred in from a small junior college, terrified for the intimidating size of Iowa State. Fortunately I have been proven wrong. The campus is easy to navigate and easily access different areas of campus through the bus system. The professors and academic advisors have gone above and beyond with connecting with students and making them feel at home. The problems that I see on campus involve advertising student events and groups. There are tons of different clubs to join on campus but not very impressive advertising for those clubs. I feel as if the college was involved in the advertisement of their wide variety of clubs, it would improve the student life immensely.
It was really good, I really enjoyed the atmosphere and the people here are extremely nice. Overall I recommend the school to anyone interested
I love the fact that professors care so much about success as a student and are willing to help no matter the situation. It feels like home and I feel so safe here.

I am happy about my decision to attend Iowa State. It was bigger yet still like a small town where I attended high school. My student advisor is always happy to help guide me in the right direction. I am doing a 3plus one program with Iowa state and palmer and between the 2 advisors making sure I am on track to be exactly where I need to be to make that happen. I have met some great friends here that I will have for a lifetime. I do not have any complaints about Iowa State. Professors are always available for help if needed. The campus is easy to navigate from class to class. Workout area is provided for students to use. I also took a job at the library to make some extra money, so I like they help students in that way as well.

I like the beautiful campus and programs offered like mine, sports media and communications. Our athletics and the Jack Trice as well as Hilton Coliseum are wonderful to go to. I also like the professors overall and feel there engaging. I do wish there was more to do on campus for non Greek students.

Iowa State University is a place where learning occurs, friends are made, and futures are found. During my time at Iowa State University, I have learned a lot in each of my classes whether they are related to my major or not because each professor cares about what they teach. I have made friends that have become like family and have had experiences like no other. Iowa State University is not just a campus; it is something that it's students can call home.

Iowa State University has hundreds of majors and minors to fit many student's interest. Along with the beautiful scenery of its campus, the small town of Ames gives one a sense of security and comfort. No matter where you go, everyone is nice, holding the doors for you and genuinely wish for the best when it concerns your future. Iowa State is also known to be extremely thoughtful when it comes to financial aid. As an out-of-state student and first generation, I am so glad I chose Iowa State to be my college away from home.
Iowa State is a good school if you are looking for somewhere that is safe, with a decent night life, and good academics. ISU is a mid-sized campus with a lot to do!
Such a good school but beware, not a ton of going out except for frats unless you’re over 21. Also a pretty place in the fall!

I like that Ames is a college town. Nearly everything revolves around the university. I like that ISU is a big school. Sporting events are great! ISU football and basketball are some of the best in the country. Greek life is pretty big at Iowa State. There are lots of Frats and Sororities, and many clubs to be involved in. Classes and professors are top-notch. Lots of students studying technical degrees. ISU really shines for IT and engineering degrees.
Iowa state university is great. The campus is beautiful and makes you feel at home. Really safe campus with university police, lot of bus, and saferide.

The campus is very pretty, but also large, which can lead to feeling overwhelmed. I also found it difficult to make friends because there are a ton of people, and for the most part, everyone is focused on themselves and achieving their goals. A plus side is that the professors are nice and want you to succeed, so they are always willing to help you; you just can't be afraid to reach out.

Iowa State was a great school to pursue design, with incredibly engaged professors and campus life. The campus is full of activities to help break up stressful weeks of tests/studying, and the big central campus acts as a great place to meet with friends for picnics or study sessions. Ames also has loads of cafes for study breaks and quiet study spots. I also worked with the outdoor rec program while studying and found the balance with school work, campus life, and extracurriculars very manageable. Would highly recommend Iowa State to anyone looking to pursue a design, agriculture, or engineering degree.

ai_summary: Jackson State University has received a variety of reviews that highlight its vibrant and welcoming campus atmosphere , making it feel like a home for many students. The sense of community is strong, with students and faculty described as friendly and supportive, often forming lasting friendships and a familial bond. Academically, while there are opportunities provided through a wide range of programs and dedicated professors, some reviews point out inconsistencies in teaching quality and administrative processes. The student life is rich with activities, spirit, and a focus on service, though there are calls for improvements in dorm quality and campus safety. Reviews also reflect a diversity that enriches the college experience, distinguishing JSU as a unique institution that emphasizes cultural pride and support , particularly for minority students. However, challenges with financial aid communication and housing logistics have been noted, suggesting areas for improvement despite the overall positive sentiment.

reviews.text: So far being at Jackson State University I love it campus is very clean and welcoming it’s extremely safe with 24hr security and watch in all dorms . I really enjoy campus life and faculty is very helpful and there if you need them no matter the situation .

A great college experience at Jackson State University is often defined by its strong sense of community, cultural pride, and opportunities for personal growth. As an HBCU, JSU fosters a supportive, family-like environment where students feel encouraged both academically and socially. Students benefit from engaged professors, diverse academic programs, and numerous campus organizations that promote leadership, service, and involvement. Traditions such as football games and performances by the renowned Sonic Boom of the South marching band bring students together and strengthen school spirit. Overall, many students describe JSU as a place that not only prepares them for their careers but also helps them build lifelong friendships, confidence, and a deep sense of identity.

Attending JSU was probably one of my best decisions. There are many opportunities pertaining to my Electrical engineering major and very helpful resources available if I need them. I am also apart of one of the world's greatest college bands at JSU The "Sonic Boom Of The South". We travel to many different places for football games and band battles and the experience is top tier. All in all, JSU was one of my best choices when choosing a college.
It is THEE I LOVE. The School I have known I wanted to attend. Like all colleges/universities, it is not perfect, but it is THEE Jackson State and there is no other.

Jackson State University is the first school that I have attended for summertime. The school has it pros and cons but it the greatest school to attend. During the summer my friend and I went on several trips. The trip was exciting and fun with my instructor on board. During my first year my teacher was very supportive and nice. The only problem occurred was that we had curriculum scheduled often. The school offered better food this year than last year. Attending Jackson State open my eyes to many different adventures. The things that is need for changes is less time completing curriculum. My greatest accomplishment was learning how to make things happen for myself and others.
One thing I’d like to see change is more opportunities for online students, there’s so many things I wish I could do and be a part of but things are very limited for non traditional students.

Jackson State University is a great school with a welcoming, family-oriented environment that makes it easy for students to feel at home. Academically, JSU offers strong programs and supportive professors who genuinely want to see students succeed, while still challenging them to grow and reach their full potential. Outside the classroom, the campus stays lively with plenty of activities, organizations, and events that help students connect, relax, and enjoy their college experience. Whether it’s attending games, joining clubs, participating in Greek life, or taking part in community service, JSU gives students countless opportunities to get involved and build lifelong friendships. Overall, Jackson State is a fun, motivating, and growth-centered place where students can develop both academically and personally.

my overall experience has been a mix of excitement, learning, and finding my place. The campus felt welcoming from day one — orientation helped me meet other new students, learn my way around, and get comfortable with campus resources. I appreciated how easy it was to find student groups and activities that match my interests, which made it less intimidating to make friends and feel connected.
Academically, the classes are challenging but manageable. My professors care and are approachable; many hold regular office hours and encourage questions. The class sizes vary — some intro lectures are large, but smaller discussion sections and labs make it easier to engage and get feedback. I’ve also started using the academic support centers on campus (tutoring, writing center), and they’ve been really helpful when I need extra clarification

My experience was life changing. Jackson State gave me the opportunity to feel independent while still having a support system. Although the university is an amazing atmosphere, the surrounding environment needs improvement.

Attending Jackson State University has been a transformative and enriching experience. One of the things I appreciated most was the strong sense of community and culture. The university’s rich history and commitment to excellence, especially as an HBCU, created an environment that was both empowering and inspiring. Faculty members were passionate about student success, and many professors went above and beyond to offer mentorship and academic support. The vibrant campus life and student organizations made it easy to get involved and feel connected. JSU’s pride in its traditions and heritage fosters a deep sense of belonging.

I completely love Jackson state the campus is so much fun. The food in the student center is always great. I love my dorm room it’s spacious and comfortable I’m an engineering major and I find that the professors at Jackson state can be a bit hard especially the fact that I am taking all science and mathematics courses overall I have to say that attending Jackson state has
When it comes down to JSU contact numbers,they need to fix their phone lines because that causes frustration for people who actually paying money out they pocket and nobody picking up

As a proud student of an HBCU, I expected support, unity, and understanding, not silence and struggle.
Jackson State has mistreated its upperclassmen, removing housing assignments just two weeks before move-in, forcing students many from hours away to come up with over $6,000 to live in hotels with no meal plans and no kitchen access. For students like me, who are independent with no financial support, this is not just stressful it's unsustainable.
We are being overlooked, unheard, and unsupported.
Emails go unanswered. Phones don’t get picked up.
As an HBCU, Jackson State should be a place where we uplift each other, where student success is a community effort, not a privilege only some can afford. HOUSING IS A NEED, NOT A LUXURY
Right now, it doesn’t feel like we matter.
We need to be seen. We need to be heard.
We need real solutions, not last-minute changes that put our futures at risk.
Please stand with us. Please help us. We deserve better.
My first day at thee Jackson state university was pretty good the people that helped me into my dorm was very nice. The one thing I would say they need to change is the Wi-Fi.

s an incoming freshman, my experience thus far has been mixed emotions for example, excitement and unknown. As the majority of my answers in the survey were educated guesses, I haven’t fully started yet. One thing that I enjoyed is the energy and pride that I feel on campus which makes me feel like I belong to something special. That being said, I did want better service with the housing process. The communication has been very last minute, so it is hard for students or families to plan. If we could communicate sooner, or more clearly, that would be helpful.

ai_summary: James Madison University has a vibrant and welcoming campus that many students describe as feeling like home. The beautiful surroundings and strong sense of community are frequently highlighted, with a supportive student body and dedicated professors who genuinely care about student success. Academically, JMU offers a wide variety of majors and classes, often in small sizes, providing a more personalized learning experience . However, students express concerns about diversity on campus and note that resources can be unevenly distributed, favoring athletics over other organizations. While the social scene is lively, characterized by a party culture , there are also many opportunities for involvement in clubs and extracurricular activities. Overall, JMU is noted for its exceptional dining options , friendly atmosphere, and a strong network of support, making it a popular choice among students.

reviews.text: I had such an amazing experience at JMU. The students are all so friendly and driven, and always willing to help out a fellow Duke. I had a great balance of academics, campus involvement, and fun.

Having transferred out of this university as a freshman, no one should go here as an out-of-state person who actually cares about academics. This place has awful support systems, horrific professors, especially for GVPT, and overall is a place I would never recommend for people who are looking for an education. If you are party-heavy, this place is good for that, but if you have aspirations for an academic life after, I would never recommend this place. Also, being here out of state, paying 40,000 would never be a mistake I make again. Glad to be far away from this place and its professors, and I would stay clear, especially as an out-of-state person. Food is good, campus life is fun, but if you struggle at all they do not have a support system that is willing to actually help you. Go to community college for 2 years and transfer to UVA, this place is not worth the money.

I like the overall atmosphere minus the fact that these universities are exploiting people for profit, and there was an ad on Spotify that pmo. so don't go here
:)

My experience at James Madison University has been both enriching and inspiring. From the moment I arrived on campus, I felt welcomed into a supportive and vibrant community.
The College of Business at JMU stands out in particular. The program is career focused. It has been a very positive and rewarding experience. It has helped me grow not only as a student but also as a professional and as an individual. I am proud to be part of JMU community.

I love being a student at James Madison University! I'm currently a freshman elementary education major and there is such a different approach used in classes compared to other schools. We not only learn what we need too, but why we need what we learn. The food is amazing, the people are great, and the professors care.

My overall experience at JMU has been good. Despite some aspects if the school I believe to have been overhyped- such as the food- some parts are just as lovely as described. All of the people are extremely kind and all the professors are passionate about what they teach. The campus is wonderful, and I know that four years here would have been worth it.

The only people genuinely enjoying this school are those more interested in the campus/party scene than academics. The housing is mid-tier at best (off-campus is equally as bad unless you spend an insane amount), professors are unprofessional with only a few being actually passionate about their classes, and the people here are even worse. I have tried volunteering, working as a RA, joining multiple organizations but opportunities for career excellence are scarce and the people make the experience terrible. If you are non-white you will face racism or prejudiced remarks either from other students or from locals in Harrisonburg. The school will also do the bare minimum for your concerns regarding mental health unless you mention ideations where they will just send you to the local hospital where the treatment is beyond unbelieveably unprofessional. If you do not mind being financially confined to a greedy, incompetent school, JMU is the place for you. But if you have any dignity, avoid.

To be honest, JMU is not at all bad. I personally have not had the best experience, but that doesn't mean that you might. If you aren't on the more generic side, then you might have a harder time making friends or enjoying student life. The professors have been great so far, and academics overall have been proven themselves to be rigorous but entertaining. Campus food isn't anything fancy, but it is pretty good. Dorms/housing on the other hand isn't bad, some rooms are small, others are big, but you have what you need.
I went to volleyball camp with my high school team for 3 years and had a great experience, exploring the beautiful campus and meeting leaders
I went to a volleyball camp with my high school team. I liked the staff; everyone was super nice and welcoming. I don't think there's anything I would change. I enjoyed my time there.

School focused heavily on athletics and student life and experience. They place a lot of emphasis on the food and campus and other programs to get people in the door and keep students happy. However, this causes a lack of focus on more specialized academic programs.

I recently finished my first semester at JMU and I love it! The community is welcoming and the campus has a very positive atmosphere. I have enjoyed dorm life and getting to know new people. There are plenty of class options and there is a great program for my major (nursing). There are also many activities to participate in around campus, a good amount of tasty food options, and nice facilities.
Very flexible especially for working students. Great support from professors and IT. Easy orientation

My overall experience with James Madison University had been excellent. They do an amazing job mixing student and life and academics. I felt right at home when I first stepped on campus. The campus is also kept up with amazingly.

Do not come to JMU. It is a greedy school more focused on football than academics. The people are judgmental and rude, looking extended high school experience. The food is not good contrary to popular belief, and the parties are lame. If you are not white be prepared for a lottt of white racist people. If you loved highschool JMU is the place for you, otherwise look literally anywhere else.

ai_summary: Johns Hopkins University offers a strong academic environment that many students appreciate for its rigor and the wealth of research opportunities available. Students often highlight the exceptional quality of professors and the support systems in place, which help them thrive academically. While the university's focus is primarily on academics, student life can feel competitive and less socially vibrant compared to other colleges, with some students wishing for more opportunities to unwind and socialize outside of a rigorous workload. The surrounding area of Baltimore is noted for its cultural offerings , but concerns about safety and the general atmosphere vary among students. Admissions and financial aspects, particularly tuition, are also points of discussion, with some weighing the costs against their experiences. Overall, the community is recognized for its intellectual passion , but it can sometimes lack a cohesive social scene.

reviews.text: I have had a pleasant experience with Johns Hopkins University as the employees are very supportive and make sure to answer any questions that I have.

Everyone starts out as pre-med, so it could be intimidating at first. But around sophomore/junior year, people tend to figure out what they really want to do. Everyone is extremely motivated to do well here. Lot of school resources to help students thrive academically

Great rigor in academics that can really build character in time management and ability to handle pressure. It does feel like trying to survive a pressure cooker though as everyone around you is just as (if not even more so) locked in, talented, and ambitious.

Choosing Johns Hopkins was the worst decision of my life. The courses were too easy and the professors didn't teaching anything. On top of that, the cafeteria was atrocious! Sometimes I even saw mold in my mac-n-cheese! Overall DON'T go to this college, or else you will experience the same atrocities as I did. 1/10 stars.
I havent gone yet but I am very excited. I am going for womens lacrosse and am so happy to know I am going to a high academic school with great people and community.

Overall, the Johns Hopkins experience is described as intellectually rigorous yet supportive, offering incredible research/academic opportunities, a diverse global community, vibrant campus life with city access, and transformative personal growth, with students praising engaging professors, driven peers, and chances to build lifelong connections through academics, and clubs.

I went to Johns Hopkins for my B.S. in Materials Science and Engineering, and I am now staying to pursue an M.S. in Regenerative and Stem Cell Technologies. Hopkins has a plethora of research opportunities and ways to get involved with the community, which I value highly.

I really suffered at this school. I went there with so many hopes and dreams but due to a combination of COVID craziness, abysmal workload (thanks ChemBE), and just a generally weird social scene, I didn't have the experience I had hoped for. Dating was brutal. Parties nearly non existent. Had some close buddies which was cool but not a lot fun to do in the area. Might better now but when I was there it was really unsafe. Clubs and sports and organizations didn't have a lot going on when I was there during COVID. Really regrettable time. On the flip side, it got me ready for my career and I'm doing relatively well for myself.
Great school with great academics. Students are very driven, but that does lend itself to a very competitive, cut-throat atmosphere.

Johns Hopkins has been an incredible place to grow, with a perfect balance of academic rigor and vibrant campus life. The community is supportive and comprised of driven individuals who inspire one another every day. The coursework challenges you intellectually but also helps you discover who you are and how to thrive beyond Hopkins.
havent been there yet since it is my first yeat in grad school. but i am excited to explore the campues and the students there
Every advisor I met was extremely helpful, especially those related to financial aid and course registration. I would like to see the COA go down slightly because I am not too financially well off

Studying at Johns Hopkins University has been a transformative experience. The academic rigor, global perspective, and dedication to solving real-world health problems are unparalleled. I’ve particularly appreciated the school’s commitment to equity and evidence-based public health, as well as the opportunity to learn from leading experts in the field. The collaborative environment fosters not only intellectual growth but also a sense of purpose and impact.
What I would love to see improved is broader financial support for international students, especially those from low- and middle-income countries. Expanding mentorship and networking programs beyond campus, particularly in global health policy and implementation, could also further empower students to drive change in their home countries.

Well, I am only starting out with Johns Hopkins University. Everyone has been very helpful and understanding.
If anything I would just like to see, with the program I'm in, that more JHU financial assistance would be available for it. For, I am a public school teacher. And, my job requires me to obtain a Masters Degree. My home state doesn't have the major I'm looking for. But, Johns Hopkins does. But, I can't leave me home state to take classes at Johns Hopkins. But, I can take all the classes I need at Johns Hopkins online.
However, Johns Hopkins tells me that the program that I'm in, JHU financial assistance isn't allowed. Which I won't argue at all. If that's the way they have it set up, that's perfectly understandable. They inform me that most of their students get financial support from their employers for the tuition. But, I am going to get no financial support for my tuition. That's why I need to apply for third-party scholarships.

Academics and professors are really great overall. I’m really happy here and have a great work life balance. The university area isn’t as unsafe as people make it out to be. The only bad parts are the food and constant construction.
Judson College at Southeastern is a great school. Students and Faculty are very welcoming and knowledgeable.
Great professors that want you to succeed and are constantly praying for their students. So many friendly students!

Judson College at Southeastern is an amazing place if you are seeking a Christian education and want to be built up and encouraged by a community of Christians. There is a huge focus on evangelism and the importance of telling others about the hope of Jesus and every person I have met here has been kind, uplifting, Jesus-loving, and enjoyable to be around.
Judson College at Southeastern is a wonderful and very engaging school. I've enjoyed my time there so far!
I’ve taken dual enrollment 2 years of high school at Judson and loved it so much that I’m starting college there this fall.

Judson excels at providing a safe, Christian environment while also finding multiple ways for people to have fun and socialize. There is always some form of event going on that, if you have time, you show up to.
The professors are incredibly engaging, and they really pursue relationships in their classrooms. The balance of professionalism, and care is done very well.
Judson is also just a beautiful campus. With all of the old buildings and manicured landscaping, while also sitting next to historic downtown Wake Forest, You can't help but enjoy the atmosphere and beauty.

Judson College at Southeastern is a wonderful option if you want Christ to be the center of your college career. All the professors and staff are super nice and helpful with any questions you may have. I wouldn’t want to change anything about Judson College at Southeastern. This school is incredible!
At Judson College I felt like every classroom was a great Commission Classroom that was equipping me to to serve all of my life for Christ in either ministry, missions, or the marketplace.
Baptist Evangelical Seminary that offers robust theological degrees. Great professors and challenging academics, however little undergraduate campus life.
The campus is beautiful, the students and staff are lovely, and the community is fantastic. I have also enjoyed the sense of academic rigor shared by the professors and the students.

When I first visited the campus of Southeastern Baptist Theological Seminary I was blown away by vast majority of acceptance and love of the people on campus. I love the beautiful brick buildings and small campus life enviorment. I am super excited to start here in the fall!
I love it. I enjoyed my classes. Every day I learn something new. I'm study music education and performance. I love to sing since I'm on 5 grade at First Baptist Church at the field

It's a quite excellent experience for me being a Asian student over there. Mostly I like a friendly and warm environment, all the professor and students are so helpful and ready to help me in every steps and its a nice gesture for me, I feel i am in home. Next attractive thing is most of the class are research class, they give us a time to research as well as they give us time to express our ideas, views which help me to search more and more .

i have gone to southeastern Baptist theological seminary for two semesters now and for the most part i love it. the community is amazing the teaching is highly educational and scriptural. the only thing i would have to say against the school is that they kick out their students from the dorms during the summer semesters, and the student housing provided just isn't worth it.

Pros: Accepting of students of different nationalities and backgrounds. Supportive house system and incredible house leadership! Beautiful campus and friendly professors. Fairly good cafeteria food. God-centered college. Interesting classes. Many opportunities for missions.
Cons: No athletics, clubs, groups, or activities. Very little diversity of thought. If you *don't* like having your opinions challenged, and if you *don't* like growing and developing your mindset... then this is a good school for you. Being at Southeastern feels a bit like living in an echo chamber.

ai_summary: Kansas State University receives high marks for its welcoming atmosphere and supportive community , with many students feeling a strong sense of belonging and camaraderie. The campus is described as beautiful, clean, and easy to navigate, located in a pleasant small-town environment that encourages social interaction and involvement in various activities, from sports to clubs. Academically, most students praise the professors for their dedication and approachability, although there are some concerns about the clarity of instruction from a few faculty members. The university offers diverse academic opportunities and robust student services , though some students seek more scholarship options and specific support for particular majors. Overall, K-State is considered a good fit for those looking for a close-knit college experience with a strong focus on student success.

reviews.text: K-State is a great school and I truly believe that all of the faculty employed here want to see everyone succeed.

I wish they were more accommodating with scholarships. Student life is definitely wonderful, but if you do not have money, they are not very generous with sharing it unless you are a diverse, multicultural student.

Kansas state university is more of a relax school and great start into college life especially if your nervous for the transition. Great spots to study. Not as high diversity but there are still groups to meet more diverse people and many clubs to join.
I like the ability to make my schedule flexible and be able to communicate tons with my professors. I would like to have pickleball courts on campus and have better sports teams.

My experience at Kansas State University has been very positive overall. One of the things I value most about K-State is the strong sense of community. Even as a large university, it feels welcoming through student organizations, academic departments, and approachable professors who genuinely care about student success. I’ve appreciated the emphasis on hands-on learning and leadership opportunities, which have helped me grow both academically and personally.
That said, there are areas that could improve. Navigating resources such as advising, mental health services, and career planning can sometimes feel overwhelming, especially for newer students. Clearer communication and easier access to these supports would enhance the student experience. Overall, K-State has provided me with meaningful opportunities, supportive relationships, and a strong foundation for my future goals.

I love K state so far, im in my freshman year and I am getting academic help from the SAC center so I have more time on my exams, I can take them not in the class and honestly im glad the school can accommodate for so many students like that. I am also having a wonderful time with my professors as they are all very willing to help you and meet with you outside of class time to help you succeed. I am a student living off campus and going to school full time AND working full time and I do have to admit, my student life isn't well and I havent connected with many other students yet since many of the campus activities at at 7 or 6 and im working, I wish they did more things in the mornings to help students connect more. If your not working full time though and going to classes full time there are many connection opportunities and I urge people I know to try them.

I would say that Kansas State is a fun place to go because it feels like home. That creates its limitations in the sense that it doesn't adjust for a bigger metro-style life, but not everyone needs that. The only thing I wish would change is that there are more things for people to do that are niche or higher standard, like coffee and cake nights and stuff like that.
I really like all of my professors and how involved they are with our academics and helping us when we have questions.
Kansas State has been the absolute best experience! I have met my best friends here and would never choose another school if I had the opportunity to.

I have grown up attending Kansas State football games. The atmosphere in the stadium, as well as on campus, is incredible. I will be attending in the Fall of 2026 and I cannot wait. Kansas State has given me so many opportunities, from livestock and meats judging to meeting my roommates for freshman year. I am so excited for my next four years in Manhattan. EMAW!
So far I have noticed everyone is committed to helping you succeed. They are always available to answer questions and help guide you along your journey.

Very poor experience with the housing and dining staff, including a myriad of issues. Food is disgusting and way over priced for what it is. The majority of professors here are mediocre and are clearly more interested in doing their own research projects than ensuring that the students are learning the content. Most of the buildings still have lead paint, which is not disclosed, and are filled with mold, also not disclosed. The town of Manhattan itself has very little to do, which means most of the fun here is leaving. Stay away from this university if you can.

I didn't plan on enrolling at Kansas State when I was deciding on where to go to college for, but their tour just persuaded me to enroll here and I don't regret it. I've met some amazing people here and while the classes are pretty tough, it's definitely worth it.
I love that they offer Science majors online. It makes it so much easier for people like me who couldn't attend classes in person at a certain time.

Kansas State University is a wonderful college, with a wonderful campus and a surrounding college town. Aggieville is filled with many great stores and food spots, and the academics are just as good. All the professors are very nice and care about the students' well-being.

ai_summary: At Kean University, students generally appreciate the diverse and welcoming campus environment , noting that the faculty and staff are supportive and approachable. The campus is described as beautiful and well-maintained, with various food options, although some express the need for more diverse meal choices. Academically, students find the courses challenging yet manageable and praise the professors for their engagement and care. While many enjoy the active student life with events and extracurricular activities, some commuter students feel a lack of weekend activities once the majority go home. The affordability of Kean University is highlighted as a significant advantage, along with available scholarships and a strong support system to help students succeed. However, there are mixed feelings regarding the quality of housing and the availability of classes, particularly for certain majors. Overall, students express a sense of community and opportunity that makes their experience unique.

reviews.text: Kean University is a great school to go to. As an undergrad student, you are highly supported by both your advisors and your professors. There are also hundreds of students clubs that you can join in your free time, as well as so many job opportunities around campus.
I haven't had any issues going to Kean. I like restaurants that are close by the campus. The financial aid office is very helpful.
Kean University is so welcoming to everyone its chill, fun. There are many types of people that go to kean from different cultures
Kean was a great school for me. I worked full time while attending Kean part time on the nights and weekends for my undergraduate degree in Cell and Molecular Biolodgy.
My overall experience has been good however, I believe the dorms could be updated, dining hall food should have more options and some professors should respond to emails in a timely manner.
I go to Kean Ocean and I think it’s a great experience and very affordable compared to other schools
As an incoming freshman, I didn’t know what to expect. From the first day, I was met with nothing but good energy. This school felt right for me.
My freshman year was started on a very strong start, thanks to all the staff and faculty. I was well prepared and immersed in college life.
Kean has great diversity and many different people to learn from. There is something for everybody at Kean.

Studying at Kean University has been a transformative experience for me, both academically and personally. From the very beginning, I felt welcomed into a community that values diversity, inclusion, and student growth. The professors are not only knowledgeable in their fields, but they also care deeply about helping students succeed. Their guidance and encouragement have motivated me to challenge myself and to keep reaching for higher goals. The campus itself is full of resources, from modern classrooms to support centers that help students thrive in every aspect of their education. Beyond academics, Kean provides opportunities to build leadership skills, join clubs, and connect with people from many different backgrounds. I am proud to be part of this university because it has given me the tools to believe in myself, prepare for my career, and create lifelong friendships. Kean University is not just a school—it truly feels like a home and a place where the future begins.
It is great so far! I have the chance to play softball and field hockey here. I have met so many amazing people so far and can not wait to continue my education here!

Kean university has allowed me to continue my education, i was planning on entering the workforce after my associates, but i saw the opportunity i had in Kean to graduate with my bachelor's at an affordable price. I,m hoping to pu tmy degree to good use after this semester ends.
I am satisfied with the school and how it has been accommodating to students as technology changes, things we are able to study online or in person, or both.
Kean was very accomodating to my needs as a full time student managing a family and work. Grading has been fair and workload is reasonable.

My freshman year living on campus at Kean University was by far the best experience I have ever had. Coming all the way from South Jersey where it’s not as compact and urbanized, Kean University was a new experience for me. It felt like I was in a different state with the way the people in Northern Jersey live. From meeting new people with many different personalities to finding the girl of my dreams, many great things came from being at Kean.

ai_summary: Keiser University - Fort Lauderdale offers a unique educational experience that stands out for its one-class-per-month model , allowing students to focus deeply on a single subject at a time. Many reviews highlight the strong support from faculty and staff , with personalized attention in small class sizes making it easier for students to engage with their instructors. The university caters well to adult learners, providing flexibility with online courses that accommodate working students. While some mention financial concerns regarding tuition, the overall sentiment reflects a commitment to student success, with resources available to assist with academic and financial needs. However, experiences can vary, particularly regarding professor quality and administrative processes. Student life seems to be enriched by a diverse community , though some express a desire for additional campus amenities.

reviews.text: Online classes typically consists of students attending class now and again. Unfortunately, there are not students really attending. It was a disappointment to me because I would like to learn from my classmates as much as from my professors. Interaction allows the learning to be internalize.

I attended school full time while working full time, which required me to be very intentional with my time and energy. My classes were accelerated, creating a fast-paced learning environment where I had to quickly grasp new material and apply it immediately. Balancing work and school was challenging, but it pushed me to become more disciplined, resilient, and confident in my ability to handle demanding situations. This experience shaped my work ethic and reinforced my commitment to achieving my educational goals.
I don't go to the school yet but it seems great and I'd love to play Football there at the next level!

Attending college at Keiser University represents more than earning a degree for me it represents opportunity, growth, and the chance to build a better future. Keiser’s career-focused education, small class sizes, and supportive learning environment make it an ideal institution for students like me who are motivated to succeed academically while preparing for real-world careers. Being part of Keiser University has strengthened my commitment to education and personal development. One of the most meaningful aspects of Keiser University is the individualized attention students receive. The instructors are dedicated to student success and take the time to ensure that each student understands the material. This supportive approach has helped me build confidence, improve my academic skills, and remain motivated even when coursework is challenging. Keiser’s hands-on learning model allows me to apply what I learn in the classroom to practical situations.

Great school, the staff is amazing and they work with you.
My teacher is awesome always there help me understanding my assignments and projects. Great school go attend
Its a great school. I have learned alot in a short time frame. They really just wants you to pay and finish. Its better then most cause less work but you learn only whats important.

I have recommended Keiser University to all of my family and friends. During my enrollment process Amy took the time to answer any and all questions I had. The transition back into college was smooth.

I am incredibly grateful for my experience with Keiser University’s online program. I completed my Bachelor’s degree here and had such a positive journey that I am now pursuing my Master’s degree with them as well.
The flexibility of the online format has been essential for balancing my education with my professional and personal life. Courses are well-structured, and the learning platform is user-friendly and accessible.
Keiser University- Fort Lauderdale offers online, in person, and hands-on learning environment which I found helpful and beneficial. The instructors are dedicated to their jobs and their students.

My experience at Keiser University Fort Lauderdale has been mostly positive. I appreciate the supportive environment and the dedication of the faculty, especially in programs like Occupational Therapy, where the instructors are knowledgeable and genuinely invested in student success. The small class sizes have allowed for more personalized attention and meaningful interactions with professors and peers. I also liked the flexible scheduling options and the resources available, such as the library and career services, which have helped me balance my studies with other commitments. However, I would like to see improvements in the technology infrastructure, such as more reliable Wi-Fi on campus and updated computer labs. Additionally, better communication regarding deadlines and campus events could enhance the student experience.
My overall experience was exceptional. The staff is friendly and helpful, professors are detailed and particular but overall good.

I have had the privilege of being part of the Keiser University community for several years, first as an alumna with my Associate degree in Occupational Therapy and now as a current student working toward my Bachelor's degree in Health Science. Reflecting on my experience, I realize how instrumental Keiser has been in shaping my educational and professional journey. Throughout both my associate and bachelor’s degree programs, I have had a blend of positive experiences, challenges, and opportunities that have contributed significantly to my personal growth.
While the tuition is expensive, I feel it is worth it to focus on one class at a time so that you can master your studies and classes without feeling overwhelmed
Keiser staff helps you from the day you ask for information, they help you every steps of the way and a lot of resources are available for students.
My experience is very good since there is always someone to answer your questions, student attention is very good .

ai_summary: Kennesaw State University offers a well-rounded college experience , with a range of academics and extracurricular activities that emphasize student support and involvement. Many students appreciate the diverse selection of majors and the supportive nature of professors who are willing to help with academic challenges. While the campus is described as beautiful with helpful resources, parking seems to be a significant issue, often requiring students to arrive early for classes. Student life is enhanced by various clubs, events, and a strong sense of community, although some reviews mention challenges in making friends, especially for minority students. The dining options receive high praise, particularly the food quality , contributing to a vibrant campus life . Overall, KSU is recognized for its potential, but students express concerns about growing pains, including limited housing and administrative inefficiencies.

reviews.text:
Overall, my time at Kennesaw State University has been a mixed bag because I have experienced both attending classes on campus and being fully enrolled online. The transition from in-person learning to online classes was challenging at first because I really enjoyed being on campus and surrounded by peers who were all working toward a better future. I especially appreciated the campus environment, including the architecture, diverse community, sports, and the cafeteria with its wide variety of food options.
Adjusting to fully online learning took some time because it is not the same experience as attending classes in person, and there are fewer course options available online for my degree. Overall, however, my experience has been positive, and I am excited as I look forward to graduating this year.

I was there for only 2 semesters as a Freshman, so I can't speak on behalf of those that were there for longer than I was, but for the two semesters I was there, I enjoyed most of my professors, I liked the campus, and I got used to being there quickly. People are friendly and always willing to help, my professors were good at teaching and clearly passionate, and the dining hall on the Kennesaw campus is great. On the other hand, the Marietta on campus dining hall is not as great as the one in Kennesaw, there is a much larger Black and White student body over Asian, Hispanic/Latinx, etc, and the housing situation isn't great. There are more students than there is housing available, and parking is nearly impossible during the busy hours of campus time.

I had a great experience at KSU. With all of the development, my freshman year looked so different compared to my senior year. The parking needs some work. I had caring professors and received a solid education, which prepared me for my nursing career.

My overall experience with Kennesaw State University has been very positive based on my visits to the campus. I found the campus to be clean, modern, and welcoming, with a comfortable environment that felt supportive for students. The buildings and facilities looked well-maintained, and I could see students actively studying, attending classes, and socializing, which showed a strong sense of community. Being on campus helped me visualize myself there and made me feel more confident about the opportunities and resources available for academic and personal growth.

Overall, my experience at Kennesaw State University has been positive. The campus environment is welcoming, and there are many opportunities to get involved through clubs, events, and academic support services. I appreciate the variety of programs and the resources available to help students succeed. One thing I would like to see improved is communication about campus events and academic advising, as it can sometimes be hard to stay updated. Overall, it’s a supportive place to grow academically and socially.
When I went there the campus was very big and nice, some of the students I saw looked very prestigious and professional and the overall culture and morals of the school are amazing and exemplary!

I am currently a freshman at Kennesaw state university. I have wanted to go here for as long as I can remember and my expectations were not let down. The professors are very real and straight forward- which I love - but they are also very understanding. The campus is very beautiful especially at night. In addition, there are so many student resources to ensure success while I am here.
